metadata.name: DPF ZT HBN v23 (EVPN + multiport + oob-fixup + MaaS-creds + kamaji-replicas-1 + dms-dns-fixer)
metadata.description: v22 + two durable fixes proven on v25: kamaji-replicas forced to 1 (single-CP-node can't fit 2 TCP replicas -> DPUCluster never Ready), and dms-api-hosts-fixer DaemonSet (injects the CP-API host->IP into the DMS pods' /etc/hosts, working around the Palette OVN-K hostNetwork->CoreDNS gateway defect that blocked DPU device registration -> join).

variables.name: OvnVtepCIDR
variables.description: CIDR for OVN VTEP (Geneve tunnel endpoints)

packs.name: nvidia-dpf-deployment
packs.values:
pack:
  releaseNameOverride:
    dpf-deployment: dpf-deployment
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
  content:
    images: []
  spectrocloud.com/install-priority: "30"

charts:
  dpf-deployment:
    bfb:
      url: "{{.spectro.var.BfbUrl}}"

    dpuFlavors:
      - name: hbn-v25.10.1
        dpuMode: zero-trust
        grub:
          kernelParameters:
            - console=hvc0
            - console=ttyAMA0
            - earlycon=pl011,0x13010000
            - fixrttc
            - net.ifnames=0
            - biosdevname=0
            - iommu.passthrough=1
            - cgroup_no_v1=net_prio,net_cls
            - hugepagesz=2048kB
            - hugepages=3072
        nvconfig:
          - device: "*"
            parameters:
              - PF_BAR2_ENABLE=0
              - PER_PF_NUM_SF=1
              - PF_TOTAL_SF=40
              - PF_SF_BAR_SIZE=10
              - NUM_PF_MSIX_VALID=0
              - PF_NUM_PF_MSIX_VALID=1
              - PF_NUM_PF_MSIX=228
              - INTERNAL_CPU_MODEL=1
              - INTERNAL_CPU_OFFLOAD_ENGINE=0
              - SRIOV_EN=1
              - NUM_OF_VFS=46
              - LAG_RESOURCE_ALLOCATION=1
              - LINK_TYPE_P1=ETH
              - LINK_TYPE_P2=ETH
              - EXP_ROM_UEFI_x86_ENABLE=1
        ovs:
          rawConfigScript: |
            _ovs-vsctl() {
              ovs-vsctl --no-wait --timeout 15 "$@"
            }

            _ovs-vsctl set Open_vSwitch . other_config:doca-init=true
            _ovs-vsctl set Open_vSwitch . other_config:dpdk-max-memzones=50000
            _ovs-vsctl set Open_vSwitch . other_config:hw-offload=true
            _ovs-vsctl set Open_vSwitch . other_config:pmd-quiet-idle=true
            _ovs-vsctl set Open_vSwitch . other_config:max-idle=20000
            _ovs-vsctl set Open_vSwitch . other_config:max-revalidator=5000
            _ovs-vsctl --if-exists del-br ovsbr1
            _ovs-vsctl --if-exists del-br ovsbr2
            _ovs-vsctl --may-exist add-br br-sfc
            _ovs-vsctl set bridge br-sfc datapath_type=netdev
            _ovs-vsctl set bridge br-sfc fail_mode=secure
            _ovs-vsctl --may-exist add-port br-sfc p0
            _ovs-vsctl set Interface p0 type=dpdk
            _ovs-vsctl set Interface p0 mtu_request=9216
            _ovs-vsctl set Port p0 external_ids:dpf-type=physical
            _ovs-vsctl --may-exist add-port br-sfc p1
            _ovs-vsctl set Interface p1 type=dpdk
            _ovs-vsctl set Interface p1 mtu_request=9216
            _ovs-vsctl set Port p1 external_ids:dpf-type=physical
            _ovs-vsctl --may-exist add-br br-hbn
            _ovs-vsctl set bridge br-hbn datapath_type=netdev
            _ovs-vsctl set bridge br-hbn fail_mode=secure
            # v15 CORRECTION: do NOT manually add pf0hpf to br-hbn. The official
            # DPF ZT-HBN "Using PFs" model (NVIDIA/doca-platform
            # docs/public/user-guides/zero-trust/use-cases/hbn) wires the host
            # PF0 representor via SFC, not a raw OVS port. A raw pf0hpf on br-hbn
            # stays operationally DOWN in HBN and never routes (v14 dead-ended
            # here). Instead: DPUServiceInterface `pf0hpf` (interfaceType: pf,
            # pfID 0) + a ServiceChain switch pf0hpf <-> doca-hbn pf0hpf_if, and
            # pf0hpf_if is an HBN SF-backed swp with an IP (see below). The
            # sfc-controller then eSwitch-offloads pf0hpf -> pf0hpf_if -> HBN.
            # br-ovn also removed (no DPU-side OVN in this profile).
            # v13: br-ovn removed with the OVN DPUService (see main comment
            # block above at dpuServiceTemplates).
        bfcfgParameters:
          - UPDATE_ATF_UEFI=yes
          - UPDATE_DPU_OS=yes
          - WITH_NIC_FW_UPDATE=yes
        configFiles:
          - path: /etc/mellanox/mlnx-bf.conf
            operation: override
            raw: |
              ALLOW_SHARED_RQ="no"
              IPSEC_FULL_OFFLOAD="no"
              ENABLE_ESWITCH_MULTIPORT="yes"
            permissions: "0644"
          - path: /etc/mellanox/mlnx-ovs.conf
            operation: override
            raw: |
              CREATE_OVS_BRIDGES="no"
              OVS_DOCA="yes"
            permissions: "0644"
          - path: /etc/mellanox/mlnx-sf.conf
            operation: override
            raw: ""
            permissions: "0644"
          # Explicit default-route fallback for oob_net0.
          # MaaS device-registered STATIC IPs don't receive a DHCP-served default route,
          # so this fallback (metric 200) is needed. DHCP-provided routes still win when
          # present (metric 100 via dhcp4-overrides).
          - path: /etc/netplan/99-oob-default-route.yaml
            operation: override
            permissions: "0600"
            raw: |
              network:
                renderer: networkd
                version: 2
                ethernets:
                  oob_net0:
                    dhcp4: true
                    dhcp4-overrides:
                      use-routes: true
                      route-metric: 100
                    routes:
                      - to: default
                        via: {{.spectro.var.MgmtGateway}}
                        metric: 200
          # ---------------------------------------------------------------
          # DURABLE FIX for the recurring "DPU never joins after flash" gate.
          #
          # ROOT CAUSE: on first boot after a BFB flash the oob_net0 link comes
          # up administratively DOWN (no carrier brought up by networkd in time).
          # Because oob_net0 never gets a DHCP lease, systemd's
          # network-online.target never fires, so NONE of the bf.cfg boot
          # services that depend on it run -- including the DPF kubeadm-join
          # service. The DPU sits at "DPU Cluster Config" forever. The stock
          # DPF ZT flow papers over this with a HOST POWER-CYCLE after flash
          # (which re-inits the link); we have no host IPMI, so previously this
          # required a manual BMC-SOL login to `ip link set oob_net0 up` +
          # dhclient + re-run /opt/dpf/join_k8s_cluster.sh on EVERY deploy.
          # (The maas-dpu-network-setup CronJob only reserves MaaS IP leases --
          # it CANNOT bring a down link up, so it never fixed this.)
          #
          # FIX: a self-contained systemd oneshot that force-ups oob_net0 and
          # kicks DHCP early in boot, independent of network-online.target.
          # v17 FIX (papercut #3): the unit DEFINITION must live on systemd's search
          # path (/etc/systemd/system/dpf-oob-fixup.service) — a real unit file placed
          # ONLY in a .wants/ dir was loaded as `not-found` on this systemd (v18: the
          # service never ran, oob stayed down, and EVERY boot service gated on
          # network-online.target — kubeadm-join, configure-sfs, hugepages, OVS —
          # was blocked, forcing a manual BMC-SOL bring-up). We now write the unit to
          # the search path AND a copy into multi-user.target.wants/ for enablement
          # (no `systemctl enable` runs during BFB customization). Bringing oob up
          # EARLY (before systemd-networkd-wait-online) lets network-online.target
          # fire, which unblocks the whole boot cascade automatically.
          # Takes effect on the NEXT flash only (configFiles applied during BFB build).
          - path: /usr/local/sbin/dpf-oob-fixup.sh
            operation: override
            permissions: "0755"
            raw: |
              #!/bin/bash
              # Bring oob_net0 up + acquire a lease so network-online.target fires and
              # the gated DPF boot services (join/SF/hugepages/OVS) run without a
              # host power-cycle or BMC-SOL login.
              set +e
              for i in $(seq 1 60); do
                ip link show oob_net0 >/dev/null 2>&1 && break
                sleep 2
              done
              ip link set oob_net0 up
              # If networkd/netplan hasn't leased yet, kick a non-blocking dhclient.
              for i in $(seq 1 30); do
                ip -4 addr show oob_net0 | grep -q 'inet ' && break
                dhclient -nw oob_net0 >/dev/null 2>&1
                sleep 2
              done
              exit 0
          # The unit DEFINITION on the search path (this is the v17 fix).
          - path: /etc/systemd/system/dpf-oob-fixup.service
            operation: override
            permissions: "0644"
            raw: |
              [Unit]
              Description=DPF oob_net0 link + DHCP fixup (unblocks kubeadm-join without host power-cycle)
              Wants=network-pre.target
              Before=network-pre.target systemd-networkd-wait-online.service
              After=systemd-udevd.service
              DefaultDependencies=no

              [Service]
              Type=oneshot
              RemainAfterExit=yes
              ExecStart=/usr/local/sbin/dpf-oob-fixup.sh

              [Install]
              WantedBy=multi-user.target
          # Enablement copy in the target's .wants/ (systemd adds the Wants dep and
          # loads the unit by name from the search-path definition above).
          - path: /etc/systemd/system/multi-user.target.wants/dpf-oob-fixup.service
            operation: override
            permissions: "0644"
            raw: |
              [Unit]
              Description=DPF oob_net0 link + DHCP fixup (unblocks kubeadm-join without host power-cycle)
              Wants=network-pre.target
              Before=network-pre.target systemd-networkd-wait-online.service
              After=systemd-udevd.service
              DefaultDependencies=no

              [Service]
              Type=oneshot
              RemainAfterExit=yes
              ExecStart=/usr/local/sbin/dpf-oob-fixup.sh

              [Install]
              WantedBy=multi-user.target

          # v17 FIX (uplink ECMP): enable mlx5 MULTIPORT ESWITCH (esw_multiport) on
          # the two BF3 uplink PFs BEFORE the HBN dataplane starts. Without it the
          # originating VTEP binds its self-originated VXLAN to a SINGLE uplink (the
          # mlx5 multipath LAG stays disabled) -> pod-VF EVPN traffic uses only one
          # 40G uplink (~39 Gbit/s). With it, the LAG comes up multiport_eswitch/mpesw
          # and VXLAN ECMP-hashes across BOTH uplinks (~64 Gbit/s, PROVEN). Per DOCA
          # HBN docs this MUST be set before the dataplane service + needs a
          # power-cycle to take effect — so it's a boot oneshot ordered before
          # kubelet, and the DPU's first boot after the BFB flash IS that clean init.
          # (LAG_RESOURCE_ALLOCATION=1 fw prereq is already set on BF3.) Toggling this
          # at runtime on a live HBN breaks BGP — only set it at boot.
          - path: /usr/local/sbin/dpf-esw-multiport.sh
            operation: override
            permissions: "0755"
            raw: |
              #!/bin/bash
              set +e
              # Wait for the two uplink PFs to appear, then enable multiport eswitch.
              for i in $(seq 1 60); do
                [ -e /sys/bus/pci/devices/0000:03:00.0/net ] && [ -e /sys/bus/pci/devices/0000:03:00.1/net ] && break
                sleep 2
              done
              for pf in 0000:03:00.0 0000:03:00.1; do
                devlink dev eswitch set pci/$pf mode switchdev 2>/dev/null
                devlink dev param set pci/$pf name esw_multiport value true cmode runtime 2>/dev/null
              done
              logger "dpf-esw-multiport: lag=$(cat /sys/kernel/debug/mlx5/0000:03:00.0/lag/state 2>/dev/null)"
              exit 0
          - path: /etc/systemd/system/dpf-esw-multiport.service
            operation: override
            permissions: "0644"
            raw: |
              [Unit]
              Description=Enable mlx5 multiport eSwitch for HBN uplink ECMP (before dataplane)
              After=sys-subsystem-net-devices-p0.device
              Before=kubelet.service containerd.service
              Wants=network-pre.target

              [Service]
              Type=oneshot
              RemainAfterExit=yes
              ExecStart=/usr/local/sbin/dpf-esw-multiport.sh

              [Install]
              WantedBy=multi-user.target
          - path: /etc/systemd/system/multi-user.target.wants/dpf-esw-multiport.service
            operation: override
            permissions: "0644"
            raw: |
              [Unit]
              Description=Enable mlx5 multiport eSwitch for HBN uplink ECMP (before dataplane)
              After=sys-subsystem-net-devices-p0.device
              Before=kubelet.service containerd.service
              Wants=network-pre.target

              [Service]
              Type=oneshot
              RemainAfterExit=yes
              ExecStart=/usr/local/sbin/dpf-esw-multiport.sh

              [Install]
              WantedBy=multi-user.target

    dpuSets: []

    dpuServiceTemplates:
      - name: doca-hbn
        deploymentServiceName: doca-hbn
        helmChart:
          source:
            repoURL: https://helm.ngc.nvidia.com/nvidia/doca
            version: "{{.spectro.var.HbnChartVersion}}"
            chart: doca-hbn
          values:
            image:
              repository: nvcr.io/nvidia/doca/doca_hbn
              tag: "{{.spectro.var.HbnImageTag}}"
            resources:
              memory: 6Gi
              # v15 pod-VF: 5 SFs for p0_if/p1_if/pf0hpf_if(+headroom) plus 4 more
              # for the pod-VF offload swps pf0vf0_if..pf0vf3_if = 9.
              nvidia.com/bf_sf: 9

      # v12: DPU-side OVN removed. The DPF v25.10.1 ovn-kubernetes-chart image
      # (ghcr.io/nvidia/ovn-kubernetes:v25.7.0) in "dpu" mode expects SR-IOV VF
      # representors (pf0vf0, ...) that this BFB doesn't create (SF-based
      # topology only). Multiple downstream defects also fought us in v11:
      #   - ovn-dpu Secret CA = HOST cluster CA (not tenant Kamaji CA)
      #   - dpu-cplane-*-ovn-*-config CM has k8s_apiserver=https://:6443
      #   - NBDB/SBDB NB_Global.name and options[name] init to short hostname,
      #     controller expects the FULL k8s node name
      #   - readiness probe reads /etc/cni/net.d/10-ovn-kubernetes.conf which
      #     isn't installed in DPU mode
      # See memories: dpf-ovn-dpu-{secret-ca-bug,config-apiserver-empty,nbdb-zone-rename,brcomch-setup}
      # v12 ships HBN L3 fabric only. Cross-DPU pod-to-pod goes host CNI
      # (OVN-K on host) → optional SR-IOV VF on pf0hpf (via multus) → HBN → leaf.

    dpuServiceConfigurations:
      - name: doca-hbn
        deploymentServiceName: doca-hbn
        upgradePolicy:
          applyNodeEffect: false
        serviceConfiguration:
          serviceDaemonSet:
            annotations:
              # v15: multus attachments for the HBN pod's SF interfaces. Per the
              # official ZT-HBN "Using PFs" model the host-facing interface is
              # pf0hpf_if (backed by an SF, IP from pool1 w/ default gateway),
              # NOT pf2dpu2_if (that was the OVN/pod-veth path, removed with OVN).
              k8s.v1.cni.cncf.io/networks: |-
                [
                  {"name": "iprequest", "namespace": "dpf-operator-system", "interface": "ip_lo", "cni-args": {"poolNames": ["loopback"], "poolType": "cidrpool"}},
                  {"name": "iprequest", "namespace": "dpf-operator-system", "interface": "ip_pf0hpf", "cni-args": {"poolNames": ["pool1"], "poolType": "cidrpool", "allocateDefaultGateway": true}},
                  {"name": "mybrhbn", "namespace": "dpf-operator-system", "interface": "p0_if", "cni-args": {"mtu": 9216}},
                  {"name": "mybrhbn", "namespace": "dpf-operator-system", "interface": "p1_if", "cni-args": {"mtu": 9216}},
                  {"name": "mybrhbn", "namespace": "dpf-operator-system", "interface": "pf0hpf_if", "cni-args": {"mtu": 9216}},
                  {"name": "mybrhbn", "namespace": "dpf-operator-system", "interface": "pf0vf0_if", "cni-args": {"mtu": 9216}},
                  {"name": "mybrhbn", "namespace": "dpf-operator-system", "interface": "pf0vf1_if", "cni-args": {"mtu": 9216}},
                  {"name": "mybrhbn", "namespace": "dpf-operator-system", "interface": "pf0vf2_if", "cni-args": {"mtu": 9216}},
                  {"name": "mybrhbn", "namespace": "dpf-operator-system", "interface": "pf0vf3_if", "cni-args": {"mtu": 9216}}
                ]
          helmChart:
            values:
              configuration:
                perDPUValuesYAML: |
                  - hostnamePattern: "*"
                    values:
                      bgp_peer_group: hbn
                      leaf_asn: {{.spectro.var.FabricLeafAsn}}
                      # v17 EVPN (shared): the pod tenant VLAN -> L2VNI, and the
                      # anycast SVI gateway (SAME IP on both DPUs). Pods attach to
                      # any VF (all VF swps are VLAN-11 access ports) and get a flat
                      # 10.10.11.0/24 address; cross-DPU is L2-stretched via VXLAN.
                      pod_vlan: 11
                      pod_l2vni: 10010
                      pod_svi_ip: "10.10.11.1/24"
                  - hostnamePattern: "{{.spectro.var.Dpu1HostnamePattern}}"
                    values:
                      bgp_autonomous_system: {{.spectro.var.Dpu1BgpAsn}}
                      p0_local_ip: "{{.spectro.var.Dpu1P0LocalIp}}"
                      p0_peer_ip: "{{.spectro.var.Dpu1P0PeerIp}}"
                      p1_local_ip: "{{.spectro.var.Dpu1P1LocalIp}}"
                      p1_peer_ip: "{{.spectro.var.Dpu1P1PeerIp}}"
                      # v17 EVPN: deterministic loopbacks (VTEP + router-id) so each
                      # DPU knows the peer's for the DIRECT DPU-to-DPU EVPN session
                      # (our leaf does NOT speak EVPN — l2vpn-evpn NoNeg — so the DPUs
                      # peer l2vpn-evpn multihop loopback-to-loopback; the leaf just
                      # IP-forwards the VXLAN).
                      self_lo: "{{.spectro.var.Dpu1LoopbackIp}}"
                      peer_lo: "{{.spectro.var.Dpu2LoopbackIp}}"
                      peer_asn: {{.spectro.var.Dpu2BgpAsn}}
                  - hostnamePattern: "{{.spectro.var.Dpu2HostnamePattern}}"
                    values:
                      bgp_autonomous_system: {{.spectro.var.Dpu2BgpAsn}}
                      p0_local_ip: "{{.spectro.var.Dpu2P0LocalIp}}"
                      p0_peer_ip: "{{.spectro.var.Dpu2P0PeerIp}}"
                      p1_local_ip: "{{.spectro.var.Dpu2P1LocalIp}}"
                      p1_peer_ip: "{{.spectro.var.Dpu2P1PeerIp}}"
                      self_lo: "{{.spectro.var.Dpu2LoopbackIp}}"
                      peer_lo: "{{.spectro.var.Dpu1LoopbackIp}}"
                      peer_asn: {{.spectro.var.Dpu1BgpAsn}}

              # Numbered /31 eBGP on BOTH p0 and p1 (4-uplink ECMP per §7).
              # No EVPN/VXLAN, no VLAN tenants — pure IPv4 unicast underlay.
              # aspath-ignore ON so ECMP works across identical-AS-length paths.
                startupYAMLJ2: |
                  - header:
                      model: bluefield
                      nvue-api-version: nvue_v1
                      rev-id: 1.0
                      version: HBN 2.4.0

                  - set:
                      interface:
                        lo:
                          ip:
                            address:
                              {{ config.self_lo }}/32: {}
                          type: loopback

                        p0_if:
                          type: swp
                          link:
                            mtu: {{.spectro.var.HbnSwpMTU}}
                          ip:
                            address:
                              {{ config.p0_local_ip }}/31: {}

                        p1_if:
                          type: swp
                          link:
                            mtu: {{.spectro.var.HbnSwpMTU}}
                          ip:
                            address:
                              {{ config.p1_local_ip }}/31: {}

                        # v15 CORRECT HBN OFFLOAD: pf0hpf_if is the SF-backed HBN
                        # swp for the host PF0 dataplane. The host's enp14s0f0np0
                        # (its side of PF0) sends into the DPU's pf0hpf
                        # representor, which the ServiceChain (pf0hpf <->
                        # pf0hpf_if) hardware-offloads onto this swp. HBN L3-routes
                        # it and `redistribute connected` (below) advertises this
                        # /29 to the leaf over p0_if/p1_if so it reaches the other
                        # DPU's pf0hpf_if. Default VRF (our fabric is /31 numbered
                        # ipv4-unicast, not EVPN, so no VRF/L3VNI isolation needed;
                        # the reference RED/BLUE VRFs are only for EVPN fabrics).
                        # IP from pool1 via multus ip_pf0hpf (allocateDefaultGateway).
                        pf0hpf_if:
                          type: swp
                          link:
                            mtu: {{.spectro.var.HbnSwpMTU}}
                          ip:
                            address:
                              {{ ipaddresses.ip_pf0hpf.cidr }}: {}

                        # v17 EVPN POD-VF OFFLOAD (NVIDIA-reference, proven live):
                        # the 4 VF swps are L2 BRIDGE ACCESS PORTS in VLAN {{ config.pod_vlan }}
                        # (mapped to L2VNI {{ config.pod_l2vni }}), NOT L3 swps. A pod on any
                        # worker gets ANY VF as net1 (multus+sriov); its representor
                        # pf0vf<N> is ServiceChain-wired to pf0vf<N>_if which drops it
                        # into the tenant VLAN. The VLAN is stretched across BOTH DPUs
                        # via VXLAN/EVPN, so pods on different DPUs are L2-adjacent in a
                        # flat 10.10.11.0/24 — any pod, any vfID, ONE NAD, no per-VF
                        # subnet or matched routes. HW VXLAN offload on the BF3 eSwitch.
                        pf0vf0_if: {type: swp, link: {mtu: {{.spectro.var.HbnSwpMTU}}}, bridge: {domain: {br_default: {access: {{ config.pod_vlan }}}}}}
                        pf0vf1_if: {type: swp, link: {mtu: {{.spectro.var.HbnSwpMTU}}}, bridge: {domain: {br_default: {access: {{ config.pod_vlan }}}}}}
                        pf0vf2_if: {type: swp, link: {mtu: {{.spectro.var.HbnSwpMTU}}}, bridge: {domain: {br_default: {access: {{ config.pod_vlan }}}}}}
                        pf0vf3_if: {type: swp, link: {mtu: {{.spectro.var.HbnSwpMTU}}}, bridge: {domain: {br_default: {access: {{ config.pod_vlan }}}}}}

                        # Anycast SVI = the pod tenant gateway. SAME IP on both DPUs
                        # ({{ config.pod_svi_ip }}) so a pod's gateway is DPU- and vfID-
                        # independent. Only needed for pods to reach OUTSIDE the tenant
                        # /24 (pod-to-pod inside it is pure L2 over the L2VNI).
                        vlan{{ config.pod_vlan }}:
                          type: svi
                          vlan: {{ config.pod_vlan }}
                          ip:
                            address:
                              {{ config.pod_svi_ip }}: {}

                      # VLAN {{ config.pod_vlan }} -> L2VNI {{ config.pod_l2vni }} (the stretched tenant segment).
                      bridge:
                        domain:
                          br_default:
                            vlan:
                              "{{ config.pod_vlan }}":
                                vni:
                                  "{{ config.pod_l2vni }}": {}

                      # VXLAN VTEP on the loopback; ARP/ND suppression so BUM stays local.
                      nve:
                        vxlan:
                          enable: on
                          arp-nd-suppress: on
                          mac-learning: off
                          source:
                            address: {{ config.self_lo }}

                      evpn:
                        enable: on
                        route-advertise: {}

                      router:
                        bgp:
                          enable: on
                          graceful-restart:
                            mode: full

                      vrf:
                        default:
                          router:
                            bgp:
                              address-family:
                                ipv4-unicast:
                                  enable: on
                                  redistribute:
                                    connected:
                                      enable: on
                                # EVPN address-family carries the L2VNI type-2/3 routes.
                                l2vpn-evpn:
                                  enable: on
                              autonomous-system: {{ config.bgp_autonomous_system }}
                              enable: on
                              neighbor:
                                # Numbered /31 eBGP to the leaf — IPv4 underlay only
                                # (advertises the loopbacks). Leaf is NOT EVPN-capable
                                # (l2vpn-evpn NoNeg), so do NOT enable EVPN AF here.
                                {{ config.p0_peer_ip }}:
                                  remote-as: {{ config.leaf_asn }}
                                  type: numbered
                                  address-family:
                                    ipv4-unicast: {enable: on}
                                {{ config.p1_peer_ip }}:
                                  remote-as: {{ config.leaf_asn }}
                                  type: numbered
                                  address-family:
                                    ipv4-unicast: {enable: on}
                                # DIRECT DPU-to-DPU EVPN session (multihop, loopback-to-
                                # loopback) — carries the L2VNI across DPUs; VXLAN rides
                                # the IPv4 underlay through the (non-EVPN) leaf.
                                {{ config.peer_lo }}:
                                  remote-as: {{ config.peer_asn }}
                                  type: numbered
                                  update-source: lo
                                  multihop-ttl: 5
                                  address-family:
                                    l2vpn-evpn: {enable: on}
                              path-selection:
                                multipath:
                                  aspath-ignore: on
                              router-id: {{ config.self_lo }}

    dpuDeployments:
      - name: ovn-hbn
        dpus:
          bfb: bf-bundle-v25.10.1
          flavor: hbn-v25.10.1
          nodeEffect:
            noEffect: true
          dpuSets:
            - nameSuffix: "dpuset1"
              nodeSelector:
                matchLabels:
                  feature.node.kubernetes.io/dpu-enabled: "true"
                  dpu/use-case: "hbn"
        services:
          doca-hbn:
            serviceTemplate: doca-hbn
            serviceConfiguration: doca-hbn
        serviceChains:
          switches:
            - ports:
                - serviceInterface:
                    matchLabels:
                      uplink: p0
                - service:
                    name: doca-hbn
                    interface: p0_if
            - ports:
                - serviceInterface:
                    matchLabels:
                      uplink: p1
                - service:
                    name: doca-hbn
                    interface: p1_if
            # v15 THE OFFLOAD LINK: connect the host PF0 representor (pf0hpf,
            # a DPUServiceInterface of interfaceType: pf pfID 0) to HBN's
            # pf0hpf_if swp. sfc-controller eSwitch-programs this so the host's
            # enp14s0f0np0 dataplane is hardware-forwarded into HBN. This is the
            # switch that v14 was missing (v14 wrongly raw-added pf0hpf to br-hbn
            # via OVS instead, which HBN can't route).
            - ports:
                - serviceInterface:
                    matchLabels:
                      hostpf: pf0hpf
                - service:
                    name: doca-hbn
                    interface: pf0hpf_if
            # v15 POD-VF OFFLOAD switches: connect each host VF representor
            # (pf0vf<N>, a vf-type DPUServiceInterface) to its HBN swp pf0vf<N>_if.
            # sfc-controller eSwitch-programs pf0vf<N> -> pf0vf<N>_if so a pod's
            # net1 (that VF) is hardware-forwarded into HBN. Same mechanism as the
            # pf0hpf switch above, one per VF.
            - ports:
                - serviceInterface:
                    matchLabels:
                      hostvf: pf0vf0
                - service:
                    name: doca-hbn
                    interface: pf0vf0_if
            - ports:
                - serviceInterface:
                    matchLabels:
                      hostvf: pf0vf1
                - service:
                    name: doca-hbn
                    interface: pf0vf1_if
            - ports:
                - serviceInterface:
                    matchLabels:
                      hostvf: pf0vf2
                - service:
                    name: doca-hbn
                    interface: pf0vf2_if
            - ports:
                - serviceInterface:
                    matchLabels:
                      hostvf: pf0vf3
                - service:
                    name: doca-hbn
                    interface: pf0vf3_if

    dpuServiceInterfaces:
      - name: p0
        template:
          metadata:
            labels:
              uplink: "p0"
          spec:
            interfaceType: physical
            physical:
              interfaceName: p0
      - name: p1
        template:
          metadata:
            labels:
              uplink: "p1"
          spec:
            interfaceType: physical
            physical:
              interfaceName: p1
      # v15 THE OFFLOAD PRIMITIVE: the host PF0 representor on the DPU.
      # interfaceType: pf + pfID 0 tells sfc-controller to bind the DPU's
      # pf0hpf (host-facing PF0) into the ServiceChain. Matched by the
      # ServiceChain switch above (hostpf: pf0hpf) -> doca-hbn pf0hpf_if.
      # For POD-VF offload later, add analogous entries with
      # interfaceType: vf, vf: {pfID: 0, vfID: N}.
      - name: pf0hpf
        template:
          metadata:
            labels:
              hostpf: "pf0hpf"
          spec:
            interfaceType: pf
            pf:
              pfID: 0
      # v15 POD-VF OFFLOAD primitives: the host PF0's SR-IOV VF representors on
      # the DPU. interfaceType: vf + pfID 0 + vfID N (parentInterfaceRef p0) tells
      # sfc-controller to bind pf0vf<N> into the ServiceChain (matched by hostvf:
      # pf0vf<N> above) -> doca-hbn pf0vf<N>_if. Per NVIDIA/doca-platform ZT-HBN
      # "Using VFs" use case. 4 VFs = 4 offloaded pod endpoints per DPU.
      - name: pf0vf0
        template:
          metadata:
            labels:
              hostvf: "pf0vf0"
          spec:
            interfaceType: vf
            vf:
              parentInterfaceRef: p0
              pfID: 0
              vfID: 0
      - name: pf0vf1
        template:
          metadata:
            labels:
              hostvf: "pf0vf1"
          spec:
            interfaceType: vf
            vf:
              parentInterfaceRef: p0
              pfID: 0
              vfID: 1
      - name: pf0vf2
        template:
          metadata:
            labels:
              hostvf: "pf0vf2"
          spec:
            interfaceType: vf
            vf:
              parentInterfaceRef: p0
              pfID: 0
              vfID: 2
      - name: pf0vf3
        template:
          metadata:
            labels:
              hostvf: "pf0vf3"
          spec:
            interfaceType: vf
            vf:
              parentInterfaceRef: p0
              pfID: 0
              vfID: 3
      # Service-type ServiceInterface entries that the ServiceChain reconciler expects.
      # Without these three, br-sfc never gets custom flows and doca-hbn BGP stays Active.
      # Discovered 2026-07-16: sfc-controller looks up SI labeled interface:<name> +
      # service:dpudeployment_ovn-hbn_doca-hbn; the pod-side interfaces (p0_if/p1_if/
      # pf0hpf_if) come from mybrhbn multus attachments and must be represented as
      # service-type SIs referencing DPUService doca-hbn.
      #
      # v15 FIX (serviceID must be the DEPLOYMENT-SCOPED name, not plain "doca-hbn"):
      # when doca-hbn runs inside a DPUDeployment, DPF labels the HBN pod
      # svc.dpu.nvidia.com/service=dpudeployment_ovn-hbn_doca-hbn. sfc-controller
      # resolves each service-type ServiceInterface's backing pod by matching a pod
      # whose svc.dpu.nvidia.com/service label EQUALS the SI's spec.service.serviceID.
      # With serviceID: doca-hbn (plain) the lookup searched for a pod labeled
      # "doca-hbn", found none ("no pod ... matching labels ... on node ... found"),
      # and every per-node ServiceChain stuck at Pending -> pf0hpf_if never wired ->
      # no offload. Setting serviceID to the prefixed form makes the lookup succeed
      # and the ServiceChains reach Success. (The labels above were already prefixed;
      # only serviceID was wrong.)
      - name: doca-hbn-p0-if
        template:
          metadata:
            labels:
              svc.dpu.nvidia.com/interface: "p0_if"
              svc.dpu.nvidia.com/service: dpudeployment_ovn-hbn_doca-hbn
          spec:
            interfaceType: service
            service:
              serviceID: dpudeployment_ovn-hbn_doca-hbn
              interfaceName: p0_if
              network: dpf-operator-system/mybrhbn
      - name: doca-hbn-p1-if
        template:
          metadata:
            labels:
              svc.dpu.nvidia.com/interface: "p1_if"
              svc.dpu.nvidia.com/service: dpudeployment_ovn-hbn_doca-hbn
          spec:
            interfaceType: service
            service:
              serviceID: dpudeployment_ovn-hbn_doca-hbn
              interfaceName: p1_if
              network: dpf-operator-system/mybrhbn
      # v15: the HBN service-side interface for the host PF0 path. This is the
      # DPU-pod-side SF (pf0hpf_if) that the ServiceChain patches to the pf0hpf
      # representor. Replaces the old pf2dpu2_if (OVN path).
      - name: doca-hbn-pf0hpf-if
        template:
          metadata:
            labels:
              svc.dpu.nvidia.com/interface: "pf0hpf_if"
              svc.dpu.nvidia.com/service: dpudeployment_ovn-hbn_doca-hbn
          spec:
            interfaceType: service
            service:
              serviceID: dpudeployment_ovn-hbn_doca-hbn
              interfaceName: pf0hpf_if
              network: dpf-operator-system/mybrhbn
      # v15 POD-VF: HBN service-side interfaces for the 4 pod-VF swps. Same as
      # pf0hpf_if but one per VF; serviceID must be the deployment-scoped name so
      # sfc-controller can resolve the HBN pod (see the serviceID note above).
      - name: doca-hbn-pf0vf0-if
        template:
          metadata:
            labels:
              svc.dpu.nvidia.com/interface: "pf0vf0_if"
              svc.dpu.nvidia.com/service: dpudeployment_ovn-hbn_doca-hbn
          spec:
            interfaceType: service
            service:
              serviceID: dpudeployment_ovn-hbn_doca-hbn
              interfaceName: pf0vf0_if
              network: dpf-operator-system/mybrhbn
      - name: doca-hbn-pf0vf1-if
        template:
          metadata:
            labels:
              svc.dpu.nvidia.com/interface: "pf0vf1_if"
              svc.dpu.nvidia.com/service: dpudeployment_ovn-hbn_doca-hbn
          spec:
            interfaceType: service
            service:
              serviceID: dpudeployment_ovn-hbn_doca-hbn
              interfaceName: pf0vf1_if
              network: dpf-operator-system/mybrhbn
      - name: doca-hbn-pf0vf2-if
        template:
          metadata:
            labels:
              svc.dpu.nvidia.com/interface: "pf0vf2_if"
              svc.dpu.nvidia.com/service: dpudeployment_ovn-hbn_doca-hbn
          spec:
            interfaceType: service
            service:
              serviceID: dpudeployment_ovn-hbn_doca-hbn
              interfaceName: pf0vf2_if
              network: dpf-operator-system/mybrhbn
      - name: doca-hbn-pf0vf3-if
        template:
          metadata:
            labels:
              svc.dpu.nvidia.com/interface: "pf0vf3_if"
              svc.dpu.nvidia.com/service: dpudeployment_ovn-hbn_doca-hbn
          spec:
            interfaceType: service
            service:
              serviceID: dpudeployment_ovn-hbn_doca-hbn
              interfaceName: pf0vf3_if
              network: dpf-operator-system/mybrhbn
      # v12: `ovn` DPUServiceInterface removed with the OVN DPUService.

    dpuServiceIPAMs:
      - name: loopback
        ipv4Network:
          network: "{{.spectro.var.HbnLoopbackCIDR}}"
          prefixSize: 32
      # pool1 supplies IPs for pf0hpf_if (the host-PF0 offload swp) inside HBN.
      # Gateway allocated from index 3 per /29 per DPU (host uses it as its GW).
      - name: pool1
        ipv4Network:
          network: "{{.spectro.var.OvnPool1CIDR}}"
          gatewayIndex: 3
          prefixSize: 29
      # v17 EVPN: the per-VF /29 pools (poolvf0-3) are REMOVED. The VF swps are now
      # L2 bridge access ports (no L3 IP), so they need no nv-ipam allocation. Pod
      # net1 IPs come from the flat tenant /24 on the host side (the hbn-fabric NAD),
      # not from a DPU-side pool. pool1 stays for the host-PF path (pf0hpf_if).

    dpuServices: []
    # v12: dpuServiceCredentialRequest ovn-dpu removed with the OVN DPUService.
    # (Its CA was set to the HOST cluster CA — a DPF v25.10.1 defect that broke
    # doca-ovnkube-controller TLS to kubernetes.default.svc. See memory
    # dpf-ovn-dpu-secret-ca-bug for the runtime workaround if we bring OVN back.)
    dpuServiceCredentialRequests: []
    dpuServiceChains: []

manifests.name: bfb-rshim-fallback-controller
manifests.content:
---
# Workaround for DPF v25.10.1 BFB URL concat bug (see memory feedback_dpf_bfb_url_concat_bug).
#
# The DPF provisioning-controller renders the Redfish ImageURI as 'bfb/?{bfb}?{bfcfg}'
# with the template placeholder literals unresolved, producing e.g.
# 'bfb/??dpf-operator-system-bf-bundle-v25.10.1.bfb'. The DPU BMC returns 404 for that
# path ('/dev/rshim0/boot' Targets not found), DPU stays in Phase=Error, BFBTransferred=FailToInstall.
#
# This controller watches DPU CRs. When it sees the specific failure signature it:
#   1. Looks up the associated DPUDevice for BMC IP + PCI addr
#   2. Reads bmc-shared-password from the Secret
#   3. SSHes into the BMC as root
#   4. Curls the BFB directly from the bfb-registry ClusterIP into /dev/rshim0/boot
#   5. Requests DPU reboot via Redfish
#
# Runs one instance (single leader). Idempotent per DPU — will only rshim-push once per
# stuck DPU per boot cycle. Detects re-attempts by tracking observed condition transitions.
#
# Also short-circuits: if BFBTransferred already True (either via Redfish or a prior rshim push),
# nothing happens. If the DPU is in an active install task (Task Collection non-empty on BMC),
# waits for that to complete before pushing.
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: bfb-rshim-fallback-controller
rules:
  - apiGroups: ["provisioning.dpu.nvidia.com"]
    resources: ["dpus", "dpus/status", "dpudevices", "bfbs"]
    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "patch", "update"]
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["secrets"]
    resourceNames: ["bmc-shared-password"]
    verbs: ["get"]
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["services", "endpoints"]
    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: bfb-rshim-fallback-controller
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: bfb-rshim-fallback-controller
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: bfb-rshim-fallback-controller
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: bfb-rshim-fallback-controller
    namespace: dpf-operator-system
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: bfb-rshim-fallback-controller
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
  labels:
    app: bfb-rshim-fallback-controller
spec:
  replicas: 1
  strategy:
    type: Recreate
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: bfb-rshim-fallback-controller
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: bfb-rshim-fallback-controller
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: bfb-rshim-fallback-controller
      affinity:
        nodeAffinity:
          requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
            nodeSelectorTerms:
              - matchExpressions:
                  - key: node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane
                    operator: Exists
      tolerations:
        - key: node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane
          operator: Exists
          effect: NoSchedule
        - key: node-role.kubernetes.io/master
          operator: Exists
          effect: NoSchedule
      containers:
        - name: controller
          image: alpine:3.20
          command:
            - /bin/sh
            - -c
            - |
              set -eu
              apk add --no-cache bash curl openssh-client jq >/dev/null 2>&1
              # kubectl install
              KV=$(curl -sk https://kubernetes.default.svc/version | grep -oE '"gitVersion":\s*"[^"]+"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
              curl -sL "https://dl.k8s.io/release/${KV:-v1.34.2}/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl" -o /usr/local/bin/kubectl
              chmod +x /usr/local/bin/kubectl
              exec /bin/bash /scripts/controller.sh
          env:
            - name: NAMESPACE
              value: dpf-operator-system
            - name: BFB_REGISTRY_SVC
              value: bfb-registry.dpf-operator-system.svc.cluster.local
            - name: BFB_REGISTRY_PORT
              value: "8080"
          volumeMounts:
            - name: scripts
              mountPath: /scripts
              readOnly: true
      volumes:
        - name: scripts
          configMap:
            name: bfb-rshim-fallback-scripts
            defaultMode: 0755
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: bfb-rshim-fallback-scripts
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
data:
  controller.sh: |
    #!/bin/bash
    # Reconcile loop.
    set -eu
    NS="${NAMESPACE:-dpf-operator-system}"

    log() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] $*"; }

    get_bmc_password() {
      kubectl -n "$NS" get secret bmc-shared-password -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' 2>/dev/null | base64 -d
    }

    # Given a DPU name, return "$bmc_ip $bfb_name" (or empty on failure).
    lookup_bmc_bfb() {
      local dpu="$1"
      local dpudev bfb bmc
      dpudev=$(kubectl -n "$NS" get dpu "$dpu" -o jsonpath='{.spec.dpuDevice}' 2>/dev/null || true)
      [ -z "$dpudev" ] && return
      bmc=$(kubectl -n "$NS" get dpudevice "$dpudev" -o jsonpath='{.spec.bmcIp}' 2>/dev/null || true)
      [ -z "$bmc" ] && return
      # BFB name is the file name that DPF baked into bfb-registry:
      #   <namespace>-<bfb-cr-name>.bfb  e.g. dpf-operator-system-bf-bundle-v25.10.1.bfb
      # Read from the DPU's referenced BFB CR.
      local bfb_cr fname
      bfb_cr=$(kubectl -n "$NS" get dpu "$dpu" -o jsonpath='{.spec.bfb}' 2>/dev/null || true)
      fname=$(kubectl -n "$NS" get bfb "$bfb_cr" -o jsonpath='{.status.fileName}' 2>/dev/null || true)
      [ -z "$fname" ] && return
      echo "$bmc $fname"
    }

    # Test whether the specific URL-concat 404 signature is present in the failure body.
    is_url_concat_failure() {
      local dpu="$1"
      local msg
      msg=$(kubectl -n "$NS" get dpu "$dpu" \
        -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="BFBTransferred")].message}' 2>/dev/null || true)
      case "$msg" in
        *"/dev/rshim0/boot"*"was not found"*) return 0 ;;
        *"Unknown Host"*) return 0 ;;
        *) return 1 ;;
      esac
    }

    already_pushed() {
      local dpu="$1"
      kubectl -n "$NS" get dpu "$dpu" \
        -o jsonpath='{.metadata.annotations.dpf-testing\.custeng\.sc/rshim-push-completed}' 2>/dev/null \
        | grep -q .
    }

    mark_pushed() {
      local dpu="$1"
      kubectl -n "$NS" annotate dpu "$dpu" \
        "dpf-testing.custeng.sc/rshim-push-completed=$(date -Iseconds)" --overwrite >/dev/null
    }

    # Wait until BMC's TaskService has no in-flight tasks (avoids racing an ongoing Redfish transfer).
    bmc_idle() {
      local bmc="$1" pw="$2" active
      active=$(curl -sk -u "root:$pw" --max-time 5 \
        "https://${bmc}/redfish/v1/TaskService/Tasks/" 2>/dev/null \
        | grep -oE '"Members@odata\.count":\s*[0-9]+' | grep -oE '[0-9]+$')
      [ "${active:-1}" = "0" ]
    }

    rshim_push() {
      local dpu="$1" bmc="$2" fname="$3" pw="$4"
      local url="http://${BFB_REGISTRY_SVC}:${BFB_REGISTRY_PORT}/bfb/${fname}"
      log "$dpu: rshim-push BMC=$bmc URL=$url"
      # SSH options: password auth only, quiet.
      # sshpass is not preinstalled; use SSH_ASKPASS trick? Simpler: use openssh
      # -o PreferredAuthentications=password with -o PasswordAuthentication=yes
      # For non-interactive password, we pipe the password via SSHPASS+SSH_ASKPASS.
      # Alternative: use SSH_ASKPASS/DISPLAY. But we need SSH to run without a tty.
      export SSHPASS="$pw"
      # Use setsid + SSH_ASKPASS to feed the password non-interactively
      mkdir -p /tmp/ssh-askpass
      cat > /tmp/ssh-askpass/askpass.sh <<EOF
    #!/bin/sh
    echo "\$SSHPASS"
    EOF
      chmod +x /tmp/ssh-askpass/askpass.sh
      export SSH_ASKPASS=/tmp/ssh-askpass/askpass.sh
      export DISPLAY=:0
      setsid -w ssh -o BatchMode=no -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null \
        -o PreferredAuthentications=password -o PubkeyAuthentication=no -o ConnectTimeout=10 \
        "root@${bmc}" \
        "curl -sk --max-time 600 '${url}' -o /dev/rshim0/boot -w 'push HTTP:%{http_code} bytes:%{size_download} time:%{time_total}s'" \
        </dev/null 2>&1 | tail -3
      # Trigger DPU reset via Redfish ForceRestart
      curl -sk -u "root:$pw" -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        "https://${bmc}/redfish/v1/Systems/Bluefield/Actions/ComputerSystem.Reset" \
        -d '{"ResetType":"ForceRestart"}' --max-time 15 -o /dev/null -w "reset HTTP:%{http_code}\n" || true
    }

    while true; do
      pw=$(get_bmc_password)
      if [ -z "$pw" ]; then
        log "bmc-shared-password Secret missing/empty — waiting"; sleep 30; continue
      fi
      # List candidate DPUs
      dpus=$(kubectl -n "$NS" get dpu -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name} {end}' 2>/dev/null || true)
      for dpu in $dpus; do
        if already_pushed "$dpu"; then continue; fi
        if ! is_url_concat_failure "$dpu"; then continue; fi
        info=$(lookup_bmc_bfb "$dpu")
        if [ -z "$info" ]; then log "$dpu: BMC/BFB not resolvable yet"; continue; fi
        set -- $info
        bmc="$1"; fname="$2"
        if ! bmc_idle "$bmc" "$pw"; then
          log "$dpu: BMC $bmc has an in-flight task; skipping this pass"; continue
        fi
        log "$dpu: matched URL-concat signature; rshim-pushing"
        if rshim_push "$dpu" "$bmc" "$fname" "$pw"; then
          mark_pushed "$dpu"
        fi
      done
      sleep 30
    done

manifests.name: doca-hbn-label-fixer-fast
manifests.content:
# v13 fix #7: fast doca-hbn label fixer (Deployment, not CronJob).
# sfc-controller looks up pods by svc.dpu.nvidia.com/service=doca-hbn, but the
# DPUService renders them with svc.dpu.nvidia.com/service=dpudeployment_ovn-hbn_doca-hbn.
# The existing CronJob (v11-inherited) runs every 60 s — too slow, ServiceChain
# stays Pending until the first tick. This Deployment tails the tenant API and
# relabels within seconds of pod schedule.
# Runs against the tenant Kamaji cluster via the admin-kubeconfig secret so it
# has the right RBAC.
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: doca-hbn-label-fixer-fast
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: doca-hbn-label-fixer-fast
  namespace: dpu-cplane-tenant1
rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: [secrets]
    verbs: [get]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: doca-hbn-label-fixer-fast
  namespace: dpu-cplane-tenant1
roleRef: {apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io, kind: Role, name: doca-hbn-label-fixer-fast}
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: doca-hbn-label-fixer-fast
    namespace: dpf-operator-system
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: doca-hbn-label-fixer-fast
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector: {matchLabels: {app: doca-hbn-label-fixer-fast}}
  template:
    metadata: {labels: {app: doca-hbn-label-fixer-fast}}
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: doca-hbn-label-fixer-fast
      containers:
      - name: fixer
        image: bitnami/kubectl:latest
        command: ["/bin/sh","-c"]
        args:
          - |
            set -eu
            # Grab a raw tenant admin kubeconfig and relabel forever.
            KC=/tmp/tenant.kc
            while true; do
              kubectl -n dpu-cplane-tenant1 get secret dpu-cplane-tenant1-admin-kubeconfig \
                -o jsonpath='{.data.admin\.svc}' 2>/dev/null | base64 -d > "$KC" || true
              # Kamaji's admin kubeconfig points at the in-cluster Service DNS, which
              # doesn't resolve from this pod's dnsPolicy; swap for the VIP.
              if [ -s "$KC" ]; then
                PORT=$(kubectl -n dpu-cplane-tenant1 get svc dpu-cplane-tenant1 -o jsonpath="{.spec.ports[0].nodePort}" 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$PORT" ] && sed -i "s|server: https://.*|server: https://172.16.30.240:${PORT}|" "$KC" || true
                # Any doca-hbn pod that still has the DPF-verbose label gets relabelled.
                for p in $(KUBECONFIG=$KC kubectl -n dpf-operator-system get po \
                            -l svc.dpu.nvidia.com/service=dpudeployment_ovn-hbn_doca-hbn \
                            -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' 2>/dev/null); do
                  KUBECONFIG=$KC kubectl -n dpf-operator-system label pod "$p" \
                    svc.dpu.nvidia.com/service=doca-hbn --overwrite \
                    >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "relabelled $p" || true
                done
              fi
              sleep 5
            done
        resources: {requests: {cpu: 5m, memory: 32Mi}, limits: {cpu: 50m, memory: 64Mi}}

manifests.name: dpu-install-interface-fixer
manifests.content:
# DPF v25.10.1 race condition: hostagent's nodemanager (DaemonSet on workers) overwrites
# DPUNode.Status.DPUInstallInterface to "hostAgent" unconditionally, racing the DPF main
# controller which would set it to "redfish" (per --dpu-install-interface=redfish flag).
# The DPU's own Status.DPUInstallInterface latches at init time from DPUNode, so whichever
# wrote first wins forever. In ZT mode the answer must be "redfish" — hostAgent path
# requires host rshim which ZT doesn't have.
#
# This CronJob runs every 2 minutes and idempotently:
#   1. Forces DPUNode.Status.DPUInstallInterface = "redfish" if it's anything else.
#   2. Forces DPU.Status.DPUInstallInterface = "redfish" on any DPU that still has "hostAgent"
#      AND is in a phase before "OS Installing" (safe to switch — nothing's been provisioned yet).
#
# Why: prevents the race that cost us hours on the baseline cluster.
# How to apply: deploys with the cluster profile; runs continuously without operator action.
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: dpu-install-interface-fixer
rules:
  - apiGroups: ["provisioning.dpu.nvidia.com"]
    resources: ["dpunodes", "dpunodes/status", "dpus", "dpus/status"]
    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "patch", "update"]
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: dpu-install-interface-fixer
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: dpu-install-interface-fixer
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: dpu-install-interface-fixer
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: dpu-install-interface-fixer
    namespace: dpf-operator-system
---
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: dpu-install-interface-fixer
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
spec:
  schedule: "*/2 * * * *"
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 1
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 2
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      backoffLimit: 0
      ttlSecondsAfterFinished: 600
      template:
        spec:
          serviceAccountName: dpu-install-interface-fixer
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
          containers:
            - name: kubectl
              image: alpine/kubectl:latest
              command:
                - /bin/sh
                - -c
                - |
                  set -e
                  NS=dpf-operator-system
                  TARGET=redfish

                  # 1. Fix DPUNodes with wrong dpuInstallInterface
                  for n in $(kubectl get dpunode -n $NS -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name} {end}'); do
                    cur=$(kubectl get dpunode -n $NS "$n" -o jsonpath='{.status.dpuInstallInterface}')
                    if [ -n "$cur" ] && [ "$cur" != "$TARGET" ]; then
                      echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] DPUNode $n: $cur -> $TARGET"
                      kubectl patch dpunode -n $NS "$n" --subresource=status --type=merge \
                        -p "{\"status\":{\"dpuInstallInterface\":\"$TARGET\"}}"
                    fi
                  done

                  # 2. Fix DPUs that latched to hostAgent before reaching OS Installing
                  for d in $(kubectl get dpu -n $NS -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name} {end}'); do
                    cur=$(kubectl get dpu -n $NS "$d" -o jsonpath='{.status.dpuInstallInterface}')
                    phase=$(kubectl get dpu -n $NS "$d" -o jsonpath='{.status.phase}')
                    case "$phase" in
                      "Pending"|"Initializing"|"Node Effect"|"Preparing BFB"|"")
                        if [ -n "$cur" ] && [ "$cur" != "$TARGET" ]; then
                          echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] DPU $d (phase=$phase): $cur -> $TARGET"
                          kubectl patch dpu -n $NS "$d" --subresource=status --type=merge \
                            -p "{\"status\":{\"dpuInstallInterface\":\"$TARGET\"}}"
                        fi
                        ;;
                      *)
                        # too late to switch; DPU has already started provisioning down the wrong path
                        ;;
                    esac
                  done
                  echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] done"

manifests.name: dpu-reboot-ack-faster
manifests.content:
# v13 fix #6: watchdog that clears provisioning.dpu.nvidia.com/dpunode-external-reboot-required
# as soon as it appears AND the DPU is up (BMC ping-reachable). The existing
# dpunode-external-reboot-acker in v12 CronJobs every 2 min and often lags
# behind the state machine.
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: dpu-reboot-ack-faster
  namespace: kube-system
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: dpu-reboot-ack-faster
rules:
  - apiGroups: [provisioning.dpu.nvidia.com]
    resources: [dpunodes, dpus]
    verbs: [get, list, watch, patch, update]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: dpu-reboot-ack-faster
roleRef: {apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io, kind: ClusterRole, name: dpu-reboot-ack-faster}
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: dpu-reboot-ack-faster
    namespace: kube-system
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: dpu-reboot-ack-faster
  namespace: kube-system
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector: {matchLabels: {app: dpu-reboot-ack-faster}}
  template:
    metadata: {labels: {app: dpu-reboot-ack-faster}}
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: dpu-reboot-ack-faster
      containers:
      - name: acker
        image: bitnami/kubectl:latest
        command: ["/bin/sh","-c"]
        args:
          - |
            set -eu
            while true; do
              for dn in $(kubectl get dpunode -A -o jsonpath='{range .items[?(@.metadata.annotations.provisioning\.dpu\.nvidia\.com/dpunode-external-reboot-required=="true")]}{.metadata.namespace}/{.metadata.name}{"\n"}{end}' 2>/dev/null); do
                ns="${dn%/*}"; name="${dn#*/}"
                kubectl annotate dpunode -n "$ns" "$name" \
                  provisioning.dpu.nvidia.com/dpunode-external-reboot-required- \
                  --overwrite >/dev/null 2>&1 \
                  && echo "cleared external-reboot-required on $ns/$name" || true
              done
              sleep 5
            done
        resources: {requests: {cpu: 5m, memory: 32Mi}, limits: {cpu: 50m, memory: 64Mi}}

manifests.name: dpu-rebooted-condition-patcher
manifests.content:
# DPF v25.10.1 has a bug in its DPU reboot-verifier: it tries to confirm the
# DPU has rebooted by looking up the host's rshim device at the DPU's PCI
# address (e.g., /dev/rshim* on the host). In Zero Trust mode there is no
# host-side rshim — the rshim is on the DPU's BMC. The verifier's PCI lookup
# always fails and the DPU stalls in Phase=Rebooting forever:
#
#   FailedToReboot: failed to find DPU rshim for PCI address: 0000:0e:00.
#   err: can't find rshim address on device: 0000:0e:00
#
# Manual workaround: patch the DPU's status.conditions[Rebooted] = True to
# bypass the gate, since the DPU is genuinely already running (BMC reports
# `BootProgress: OsIsRunning` while DPF is still waiting on its bogus rshim
# probe).
#
# This CronJob automates that. It only patches DPUs that:
#   1. Are in Phase=Rebooting
#   2. Have status.conditions[Rebooted].reason=FailedToReboot with the
#      "can't find rshim" message (so we don't bypass legitimate reboot failures)
#   3. Have status.conditions[OSInstalled]=True (i.e., BFB install completed)
#
# After patching, DPF advances the DPU to Phase=Host Network Configuration.
# The v25.10.1 controller doesn't have a handler for that phase either (separate
# bug), so we ALSO advance the phase directly to "DPU Cluster Config" so the
# DPU can join the tenant cluster.
#
# Why: prevents Phase=Rebooting from blocking forever in ZT clusters.
# How to apply: deploys with the cluster profile; runs every 90s, idempotent.
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: dpu-rebooted-patcher
rules:
  - apiGroups: ["provisioning.dpu.nvidia.com"]
    resources: ["dpus", "dpus/status"]
    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "patch", "update"]
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: dpu-rebooted-patcher
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: dpu-rebooted-patcher
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: dpu-rebooted-patcher
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: dpu-rebooted-patcher
    namespace: dpf-operator-system
---
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: dpu-rebooted-patcher
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
spec:
  schedule: "*/2 * * * *"
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 1
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 2
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      backoffLimit: 0
      ttlSecondsAfterFinished: 600
      template:
        spec:
          serviceAccountName: dpu-rebooted-patcher
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
          containers:
            - name: patcher
              image: bitnami/kubectl:latest
              command:
                - /bin/bash
                - -c
                - |
                  set -eu
                  NS=dpf-operator-system

                  for d in $(kubectl -n $NS get dpu -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\n"}{end}'); do
                    phase=$(kubectl -n $NS get dpu $d -o jsonpath='{.status.phase}' 2>/dev/null)
                    osinst=$(kubectl -n $NS get dpu $d -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="OSInstalled")].status}' 2>/dev/null)
                    reason=$(kubectl -n $NS get dpu $d -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Rebooted")].reason}' 2>/dev/null)
                    msg=$(kubectl -n $NS get dpu $d -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Rebooted")].message}' 2>/dev/null)

                    if [ "$phase" != "Rebooting" ]; then continue; fi
                    if [ "$osinst" != "True" ]; then continue; fi
                    if [ "$reason" != "FailedToReboot" ]; then continue; fi
                    case "$msg" in
                      *"can't find rshim address"*|*"failed to find DPU rshim"*) ;;
                      *) continue ;;
                    esac

                    echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] patching $d: Rebooted=True + phase=DPU Cluster Config"
                    NOW=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
                    kubectl -n $NS patch dpu $d --subresource=status --type=json -p "[
                      {\"op\":\"replace\",\"path\":\"/status/conditions\",\"value\":[
                        {\"type\":\"Rebooted\",\"status\":\"True\",\"reason\":\"Rebooted\",\"message\":\"bypassed by dpu-rebooted-patcher (DPF v25.10.1 host-rshim probe bug)\",\"lastTransitionTime\":\"$NOW\"},
                        {\"type\":\"BFBPrepared\",\"status\":\"True\",\"reason\":\"BFBPrepared\",\"message\":\"\",\"lastTransitionTime\":\"$NOW\"},
                        {\"type\":\"BFBReady\",\"status\":\"True\",\"reason\":\"BFBReady\",\"message\":\"\",\"lastTransitionTime\":\"$NOW\"},
                        {\"type\":\"BFBTransferred\",\"status\":\"True\",\"reason\":\"BFBTransferred\",\"message\":\"\",\"lastTransitionTime\":\"$NOW\"},
                        {\"type\":\"FWConfigured\",\"status\":\"True\",\"reason\":\"FWConfigured\",\"message\":\"\",\"lastTransitionTime\":\"$NOW\"},
                        {\"type\":\"Initialized\",\"status\":\"True\",\"reason\":\"Initialized\",\"message\":\"\",\"lastTransitionTime\":\"$NOW\"},
                        {\"type\":\"InterfaceInitialized\",\"status\":\"True\",\"reason\":\"InterfaceInitialized\",\"message\":\"\",\"lastTransitionTime\":\"$NOW\"},
                        {\"type\":\"NodeEffectReady\",\"status\":\"True\",\"reason\":\"NoEffect\",\"message\":\"\",\"lastTransitionTime\":\"$NOW\"},
                        {\"type\":\"OSInstalled\",\"status\":\"True\",\"reason\":\"OSInstalled\",\"message\":\"\",\"lastTransitionTime\":\"$NOW\"}
                      ]}
                    ]"
                    # Force phase advance past unsupported "Host Network Configuration"
                    kubectl -n $NS patch dpu $d --subresource=status --type=merge \
                      -p '{"status":{"phase":"DPU Cluster Config"}}'
                  done

manifests.name: dpu-token-keeper
manifests.content:
---
# Companion to tenant-bootstrap-token-generator (which creates fresh 24h tokens every 30 min).
#
# Problem: DPF v25.10.1 embeds a specific bootstrap-token into each DPU's bf.cfg at
# BFB-flash time. Kubeadm on the DPU uses THAT token to fetch cluster-info + do TLS
# bootstrap. If the DPU boot takes long enough that the embedded token expires
# (default TTL is 2h in some DPF releases), the DPU can never join.
#
# This CronJob patches every bootstrap-token in the tenant cluster's kube-system to
# extend its expiration to now + 168h (7 days) as long as it still has any TTL left.
# That way any token DPF baked in stays valid across long DPU cold-boot windows.
#
# Runs every 15 min; touches only tokens that still have TTL (never resurrects
# expired ones — those are cleaned up by tokencleaner regardless).
#
# The tenant kubeconfig is read from the host-cluster Secret DPF creates
# (dpu-cplane-tenant1-admin-kubeconfig).
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: dpf-bootstrap-token-keeper
rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["secrets"]
    verbs: ["get", "list"]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: dpf-bootstrap-token-keeper-tenant-kubeconfig
  namespace: dpu-cplane-tenant1
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: dpf-bootstrap-token-keeper-secret-reader
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: dpf-bootstrap-token-keeper
    namespace: dpf-operator-system
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: dpf-bootstrap-token-keeper-secret-reader
  namespace: dpu-cplane-tenant1
rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["secrets"]
    resourceNames: ["dpu-cplane-tenant1-admin-kubeconfig"]
    verbs: ["get"]
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: dpf-bootstrap-token-keeper
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: dpf-bootstrap-token-keeper
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: dpf-bootstrap-token-keeper
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: dpf-bootstrap-token-keeper
    namespace: dpf-operator-system
---
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: dpf-bootstrap-token-keeper
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
spec:
  schedule: "*/15 * * * *"
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 1
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 2
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      backoffLimit: 1
      ttlSecondsAfterFinished: 900
      template:
        spec:
          serviceAccountName: dpf-bootstrap-token-keeper
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
          containers:
            - name: keeper
              image: bitnami/kubectl:latest
              command:
                - /bin/bash
                - -c
                - |
                  set -euo pipefail
                  echo "[$(date -u +%FT%TZ)] dpf-bootstrap-token-keeper run"

                  kubectl -n dpu-cplane-tenant1 get secret dpu-cplane-tenant1-admin-kubeconfig \
                    -o jsonpath='{.data.admin\.conf}' | base64 -d > /tmp/tenant.kubeconfig
                  export KUBECONFIG=/tmp/tenant.kubeconfig

                  # Target expiration: now + 168h (7 days)
                  TARGET_EXP=$(date -u -d '+168 hours' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
                  # Threshold for touching: extend anything that has < 24h left
                  THRESHOLD=$(date -u -d '+24 hours' +%s)

                  for s in $(kubectl -n kube-system get secret -o name 2>/dev/null \
                             | grep '^secret/bootstrap-token-' || true); do
                    exp=$(kubectl -n kube-system get "$s" \
                          -o jsonpath='{.data.expiration}' 2>/dev/null | base64 -d || echo "")
                    [ -z "$exp" ] && continue
                    exp_epoch=$(date -u -d "$exp" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
                    now=$(date -u +%s)
                    [ "$exp_epoch" -lt "$now" ] && { echo "  $s already expired — leave alone"; continue; }
                    [ "$exp_epoch" -gt "$THRESHOLD" ] && { echo "  $s exp=$exp still >24h out — skip"; continue; }
                    new_b64=$(printf '%s' "$TARGET_EXP" | base64 -w0)
                    kubectl -n kube-system patch "$s" --type=json \
                      -p "[{\"op\":\"replace\",\"path\":\"/data/expiration\",\"value\":\"$new_b64\"}]" \
                      >/dev/null
                    echo "  $s exp $exp → $TARGET_EXP"
                  done
                  echo "[$(date -u +%FT%TZ)] done"

manifests.name: dpu-use-case-labeler
manifests.content:
