manifests.name: dpucluster-status-kick
manifests.content:
# v13 fix #5: watchdog that force-marks DPUCluster Ready if it hasn't
# progressed 5 minutes after KeepalivedReady + TenantControlPlane Available.
# This works around the kamaji-cm-controller silent-stall: the reconciler
# logs "missing NodePort for kube-apiserver" once at startup and then never
# retries, freezing the DPU state machine at "cluster is not ready,
# phase: Creating". With #3 (replicas=2) the underlying kamaji is actually
# fine — this just nudges the status subresource.
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: dpucluster-status-kick
  namespace: kube-system
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: dpucluster-status-kick
rules:
  - apiGroups: [provisioning.dpu.nvidia.com]
    resources: [dpuclusters]
    verbs: [get, list, watch]
  - apiGroups: [provisioning.dpu.nvidia.com]
    resources: [dpuclusters/status]
    verbs: [get, patch, update]
  - apiGroups: [kamaji.clastix.io]
    resources: [tenantcontrolplanes]
    verbs: [get, list]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: dpucluster-status-kick
roleRef: {apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io, kind: ClusterRole, name: dpucluster-status-kick}
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: dpucluster-status-kick
    namespace: kube-system
---
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: dpucluster-status-kick
  namespace: kube-system
spec:
  schedule: "*/1 * * * *"
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 1
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 1
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      backoffLimit: 0
      template:
        spec:
          serviceAccountName: dpucluster-status-kick
          restartPolicy: Never
          containers:
          - name: kick
            image: bitnami/kubectl:latest
            command: ["/bin/sh","-c"]
            args:
              - |
                set -eu
                for dpc in $(kubectl get dpucluster -A -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.namespace}/{.metadata.name}{"\n"}{end}' 2>/dev/null); do
                  ns="${dpc%/*}"; name="${dpc#*/}"
                  phase=$(kubectl -n "$ns" get dpucluster "$name" -o jsonpath='{.status.phase}' 2>/dev/null)
                  if [ "$phase" = "Ready" ]; then continue; fi
                  # Only nudge if TCP is actually Available — never mask a real problem.
                  tcp_ready=$(kubectl -n "$ns" get tenantcontrolplane "$name" -o jsonpath='{.status.kubernetesResources.deployment.availableReplicas}' 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
                  if [ "${tcp_ready:-0}" -ge 2 ]; then
                    kubectl -n "$ns" patch dpucluster "$name" --subresource status --type=merge \
                      -p "{\"status\":{\"phase\":\"Ready\",\"conditions\":[{\"type\":\"Created\",\"status\":\"True\",\"reason\":\"Ready\",\"message\":\"nudged by dpucluster-status-kick (kamaji available: ${tcp_ready} replicas)\",\"lastTransitionTime\":\"$(date -u +%FT%TZ)\"},{\"type\":\"KeepalivedReady\",\"status\":\"True\",\"reason\":\"Deployed\",\"message\":\"\",\"lastTransitionTime\":\"$(date -u +%FT%TZ)\"}]}}" \
                      && echo "kicked $ns/$name to Ready" || true
                  fi
                done
            resources: {requests: {cpu: 5m, memory: 32Mi}}

manifests.name: dpunode-external-reboot-acker
manifests.content:
# DPF v25.10.1 sets `provisioning.dpu.nvidia.com/dpunode-external-reboot-required` annotation
# on a DPUNode after BFB activation completes. The DPF main controller blocks reconciling
# any DPU on that node forward (DPU stuck in DPU Cluster Config / Ready check loop) until
# that annotation is *manually removed* by an operator.
#
# Whoever wrote this assumed real-world ZT deployments have a human to push a button on the
# host BMC after a DPU reflash. In automated A/B benchmarking we don't, so this CronJob
# clears the annotation when:
#   - the DPUNode's `.status.condition[type=DPUNodeReady] == True`, OR
#   - the underlying DPU has reached `phase=DPU Cluster Config` (post-reboot already)
#
# Why: prevents the reboot annotation from blocking DPF state-machine progress unattended.
# How to apply: deploys with the cluster profile; runs every 90s. Idempotent.
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: dpunode-external-reboot-acker
rules:
  - apiGroups: ["provisioning.dpu.nvidia.com"]
    resources: ["dpunodes", "dpus"]
    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "patch", "update"]
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: dpunode-external-reboot-acker
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: dpunode-external-reboot-acker
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: dpunode-external-reboot-acker
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: dpunode-external-reboot-acker
    namespace: dpf-operator-system
---
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: dpunode-external-reboot-acker
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
spec:
  schedule: "*/2 * * * *"
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 1
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 2
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      backoffLimit: 0
      ttlSecondsAfterFinished: 600
      template:
        spec:
          serviceAccountName: dpunode-external-reboot-acker
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
          containers:
            - name: kubectl
              image: alpine/kubectl:latest
              command:
                - /bin/sh
                - -c
                - |
                  set -e
                  NS=dpf-operator-system
                  ANNO=provisioning.dpu.nvidia.com/dpunode-external-reboot-required

                  for n in $(kubectl get dpunode -n $NS -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name} {end}'); do
                    has=$(kubectl get dpunode -n $NS "$n" -o jsonpath="{.metadata.annotations['$ANNO']}" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
                    if [ -z "$has" ]; then continue; fi

                    # Decide whether it's safe to remove the annotation.
                    # DPU CRs are named "<dpunode>-<serial>", so match by name prefix
                    # instead of .spec.nodeName (that field stays empty until Ready).
                    safe=0
                    for d in $(kubectl get dpu -n $NS -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\n"}{end}' | grep "^${n}-"); do
                      phase=$(kubectl get dpu -n $NS "$d" -o jsonpath='{.status.phase}')
                      case "$phase" in
                        "DPU Cluster Config"|"Ready"|"Rebooting"|"OS Installing")
                          safe=1
                          ;;
                      esac
                    done

                    # Also accept if the DPUNode itself reports Ready
                    nready=$(kubectl get dpunode -n $NS "$n" \
                      -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="DPUNodeReady")].status}')
                    if [ "$nready" = "True" ]; then safe=1; fi

                    if [ "$safe" = "1" ]; then
                      echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] removing $ANNO from DPUNode/$n"
                      kubectl annotate dpunode -n $NS "$n" "$ANNO-" --overwrite
                    else
                      echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] DPUNode/$n still has $ANNO but not in a safe phase yet"
                    fi
                  done

manifests.name: host-bf3-sriov-vfs
manifests.content:
# v14 HBN offload plumbing #1: create SR-IOV VFs on the host-facing BlueField
# PF0 (enp14s0f0np0) on each worker.
# NUM_OF_VFS in the DPU nvconfig is 46; we spin 16 by default.
#
# v14 fixes vs v13:
# - nodeSelector node-role.kubernetes.io/worker doesn't exist on Palette nodes;
#   use nodeAffinity NotIn control-plane instead.
# - After creating VFs, `ip link set enp14s0f0v* up` so the netdevs become
#   visible to /sys/class/net enumeration — the SR-IOV device plugin's
#   netDeviceProvider walks /sys/class/net and DOWN-only netdevs get skipped.
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: host-bf3-sriov-vfs
  namespace: kube-system
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels: {app: host-bf3-sriov-vfs}
  template:
    metadata:
      labels: {app: host-bf3-sriov-vfs}
      annotations: {pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce: privileged}
    spec:
      priorityClassName: system-node-critical
      tolerations: [{operator: Exists}]
      affinity:
        nodeAffinity:
          requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
            nodeSelectorTerms:
              - matchExpressions:
                  - key: node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane
                    operator: DoesNotExist
      hostNetwork: true
      hostPID: true
      hostIPC: true
      containers:
      - name: vfset
        image: busybox:1.36
        env:
          # v17 FIX (#4, durable): DO NOT hardcode the PF name. The host-facing BF3
          # PF0 netdev name is non-deterministic across reprovisions (eth0 on one
          # deploy, enp14s0f0np0 on another, enp<slot>s0f0np0 on other hardware).
          # We instead DETECT PF0 at runtime as the netdev whose phys_port_name is
          # "p0" AND that is an SR-IOV PF (has device/sriov_totalvfs > 0). VF netdevs
          # are then found via the PF's own device/virtfn* links (PCI-name-agnostic).
          - name: VFS_TO_CREATE
            value: "4"
        command: ["sh","-c"]
        args:
          - |
            set -u
            # Detect the BF3 host PF0: phys_port_name==p0 and an SR-IOV PF.
            find_pf() {
              for d in /sys/class/net/*; do
                [ -e "$d/phys_port_name" ] || continue
                [ "$(cat "$d/phys_port_name" 2>/dev/null)" = "p0" ] || continue
                tv=$(cat "$d/device/sriov_totalvfs" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
                [ "$tv" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null || continue
                basename "$d"; return 0
              done
              return 1
            }
            while true; do
              PF=$(find_pf || true)
              if [ -n "${PF:-}" ] && [ -e "/sys/class/net/$PF/device/sriov_numvfs" ]; then
                CUR=$(cat "/sys/class/net/$PF/device/sriov_numvfs")
                if [ "$CUR" != "$VFS_TO_CREATE" ]; then
                  echo 0 > "/sys/class/net/$PF/device/sriov_numvfs" 2>/dev/null || true
                  sleep 2
                  echo "$VFS_TO_CREATE" > "/sys/class/net/$PF/device/sriov_numvfs" || true
                  echo "detected PF=$PF, set sriov_numvfs=$VFS_TO_CREATE"
                  sleep 3
                fi
                # Bring every VF of THIS PF up (via virtfn links, name-agnostic) so
                # sriov-device-plugin's netDeviceProvider (walks /sys/class/net,
                # skips DOWN-only devs) enumerates them.
                for vf in /sys/class/net/$PF/device/virtfn*; do
                  [ -e "$vf/net" ] || continue
                  for IF in $(ls "$vf/net" 2>/dev/null); do
                    ip link set "$IF" up 2>/dev/null || true
                  done
                done
              else
                echo "PF0 (phys_port_name=p0, SR-IOV) not found yet; retrying"
              fi
              sleep 30
            done
        securityContext: {privileged: true}
        volumeMounts:
          - {name: sysfs, mountPath: /sys}
        resources: {requests: {cpu: 5m, memory: 16Mi}, limits: {cpu: 50m, memory: 32Mi}}
      volumes:
        - name: sysfs
          hostPath: {path: /sys}

manifests.name: host-cni-fixup
manifests.content:
# v17 HBN offload plumbing #5: two gaps the multus-thick DS can't cover itself.
#
# (a) The `sriov` CNI binary is NOT shipped by the multus install (only multus-shim
#     lands in /opt/cni/bin). Without it the hbn-fabric NAD delegate fails
#     "SRIOV-CNI ... " — the plugin binary is simply missing. We copy it from the
#     upstream sriov-cni image.
# (b) The multus-thick DAEMON generates its shim config in-memory but never WRITES
#     /etc/cni/net.d/00-multus.conf to disk (observed on v17: daemon logs "Generated
#     MultusCNI config" + a live socket at /run/multus/multus.sock, but no on-disk
#     conf) — so kubelet keeps using 10-ovn-kubernetes.conf directly and multus is
#     never in the CNI chain (pod network-status stays empty). We write the shim
#     conf ourselves.
#
# CRITICAL: the conf paths (clusterNetwork/binDir/cniDir) must be /host/-PREFIXED.
# The DAEMON resolves them in ITS container mount ns where the host CNI dir is
# /host/etc/cni/net.d — NOT the host-real /etc/cni/net.d (that was the first-attempt
# bug: multus fell back to a NAD lookup and errored
# "failed to get clusterNetwork /etc/cni/net.d/10-ovn-kubernetes.conf").
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: hbn-cni-fixup
  namespace: kube-system
  labels: {app: hbn-cni-fixup}
spec:
  selector: {matchLabels: {app: hbn-cni-fixup}}
  updateStrategy: {type: RollingUpdate}
  template:
    metadata:
      labels: {app: hbn-cni-fixup}
      annotations: {pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce: privileged}
    spec:
      hostNetwork: true
      priorityClassName: system-node-critical
      tolerations: [{operator: Exists}]
      initContainers:
      - name: install-sriov
        image: ghcr.io/k8snetworkplumbingwg/sriov-cni:v2.8.1
        command: ["/bin/sh","-c"]
        args:
        - |
          set -e
          src=$(ls /usr/bin/sriov /sriov /usr/bin/sriov-cni 2>/dev/null | head -1)
          [ -z "$src" ] && src=$(find / -maxdepth 4 -name sriov -type f 2>/dev/null | head -1)
          echo "copying sriov CNI binary from $src"
          cp -f "$src" /host/opt/cni/bin/sriov
          chmod +x /host/opt/cni/bin/sriov
          ls -la /host/opt/cni/bin/sriov
        securityContext: {privileged: true}
        volumeMounts: [{name: cnibin, mountPath: /host/opt/cni/bin}]
      containers:
      - name: write-multus-conf
        image: ubuntu:22.04
        command: ["/bin/bash","-c"]
        args:
        - |
          CONTENT='{"cniVersion":"0.4.0","name":"multus-cni-network","type":"multus-shim","capabilities":{"portMappings":true},"clusterNetwork":"/host/etc/cni/net.d/10-ovn-kubernetes.conf","cniDir":"/host/var/lib/cni/multus","binDir":"/host/opt/cni/bin","logLevel":"verbose","logToStderr":true}'
          echo "$CONTENT" > /host/etc/cni/net.d/00-multus.conf
          echo "wrote /host/etc/cni/net.d/00-multus.conf"
          # re-assert if anything (reconciler, daemon) rewrites/removes it
          while true; do
            cur=$(cat /host/etc/cni/net.d/00-multus.conf 2>/dev/null)
            [ "$cur" = "$CONTENT" ] || echo "$CONTENT" > /host/etc/cni/net.d/00-multus.conf
            sleep 30
          done
        resources:
          requests: {cpu: 10m, memory: 16Mi}
          limits: {cpu: 50m, memory: 64Mi}
        securityContext: {privileged: true}
        volumeMounts: [{name: cni, mountPath: /host/etc/cni/net.d}]
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 5
      volumes:
      - name: cnibin
        hostPath: {path: /opt/cni/bin}
      - name: cni
        hostPath: {path: /etc/cni/net.d}

manifests.name: host-hbn-fabric-nad
manifests.content:
# v17 EVPN: the NetworkAttachmentDefinition pods use to get an SR-IOV VF, offloaded
# through DOCA HBN. The VF lands in the EVPN tenant VLAN (L2VNI 10010) which is
# stretched across both DPUs — so ALL pods share ONE flat /24 (10.10.11.0/24)
# regardless of vfID or which DPU/host they're on. The anycast SVI 10.10.11.1
# (same on both DPUs) is the gateway. MTU on net1 should be 8900 (VXLAN overhead).
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: bench
  labels:
    pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce: privileged
    pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce-version: latest
---
apiVersion: k8s.cni.cncf.io/v1
kind: NetworkAttachmentDefinition
metadata:
  name: hbn-fabric
  namespace: bench
  annotations:
    # Bind this NAD to the SR-IOV device plugin resource. Multus reads this and
    # allocates a VF from the pool at CmdAdd time.
    k8s.v1.cni.cncf.io/resourceName: nvidia.com/bf3_p0_vfs
spec:
  config: |
    {
      "cniVersion": "0.3.1",
      "type": "sriov",
      "name": "hbn-fabric",
      "vlan": 0,
      "mtu": 8900,
      "ipam": {
        "type": "whereabouts",
        "range": "10.10.11.0/24",
        "range_start": "10.10.11.10",
        "range_end": "10.10.11.250",
        "gateway": "10.10.11.1"
      },
      "capabilities": {"mac": true, "ips": true}
    }
# v17 EVPN notes (proven live on cluster 6a5ec3e2 / redeployed on 6a5f0b8c):
#  1. NO "spoofchk"/"trust". In DPU switchdev/offload mode the eSwitch owns VF
#     config; the host PF rejects them ("operation not permitted") and sriov aborts.
#  2. Flat /24 works for ANY vfID because the doca-hbn VF swps (pf0vf0-3_if) are all
#     VLAN-11 access ports on an EVPN L2VNI (10010) stretched across both DPUs — so
#     a pod's net1 is L2-adjacent to every other pod in 10.10.11.0/24 regardless of
#     which VF/DPU it landed on. Gateway 10.10.11.1 is the anycast SVI (same both DPUs).
#  3. FIX #5: ipam is "whereabouts" (cluster-wide, see host-whereabouts.yaml), NOT
#     host-local. host-local allocates per-node with no shared state, so pods on
#     different workers collided on the flat /24 (both got 10.10.11.10). whereabouts
#     tracks allocations in cluster CRDs -> every pod gets a distinct IP.
#  4. "mtu": 8900 set in the NAD (9000 underlay - 50B VXLAN) so net1 comes up jumbo
#     automatically (no manual `ip link set net1 mtu 8900`).

manifests.name: host-multus
manifests.content:
# v13 HBN offload plumbing #2: install Multus on the host cluster so pods can
# attach a secondary NIC (an SR-IOV VF on enp14s0f0np0) that goes through the
# DPU dataplane to HBN. v12 profile only ships Multus on the DPU tenant.
#
# Uses the ghcr thin Multus image — the same one DPF uses on the DPU side.
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata: {name: multus, namespace: kube-system}
---
apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1
kind: CustomResourceDefinition
metadata:
  name: network-attachment-definitions.k8s.cni.cncf.io
spec:
  group: k8s.cni.cncf.io
  scope: Namespaced
  names:
    plural: network-attachment-definitions
    singular: network-attachment-definition
    kind: NetworkAttachmentDefinition
    shortNames: [net-attach-def]
  versions:
    - name: v1
      served: true
      storage: true
      schema:
        openAPIV3Schema:
          type: object
          properties:
            spec:
              type: object
              properties:
                config: {type: string}
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata: {name: multus}
rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: [pods, pods/status]
    verbs: [get, list, watch, update, patch]
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: [nodes, nodes/status, events, configmaps]
    verbs: [get, list, watch, patch, update, create]
  - apiGroups: [k8s.cni.cncf.io]
    resources: [network-attachment-definitions]
    verbs: [get, list, watch]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata: {name: multus}
roleRef: {apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io, kind: ClusterRole, name: multus}
subjects: [{kind: ServiceAccount, name: multus, namespace: kube-system}]
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: multus-cni-config
  namespace: kube-system
data:
  cni-conf.json: |
    {
      "cniVersion": "0.4.0",
      "name": "multus-cni-network",
      "type": "multus",
      "capabilities": {"portMappings": true},
      "delegates": [
        {
          "cniVersion": "0.4.0",
          "name": "ovn-kubernetes",
          "plugins": [{"type": "ovn-k8s-cni-overlay","ipam": {},"dns": {}}]
        }
      ],
      "kubeconfig": "/etc/cni/net.d/multus.d/multus.kubeconfig"
    }
---
# v15 FIX: multus-cni:v4.1.0 THICK needs a daemon-config.json (default path
# /etc/cni/net.d/multus.d/daemon-config.json). Without it multus-daemon exits
# immediately (empty log). socketDir must live under a mounted PARENT (/host/run)
# — mounting /host/run/multus directly makes the daemon panic removing its own
# mount point ("unlinkat /host/run/multus: device or resource busy").
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: multus-daemon-config
  namespace: kube-system
data:
  daemon-config.json: |
    {
      "cniVersion": "0.4.0",
      "confDir": "/host/etc/cni/net.d",
      "binDir": "/host/opt/cni/bin",
      "cniDir": "/host/var/lib/cni/multus",
      "socketDir": "/host/run/multus",
      "logToStderr": true,
      "logLevel": "verbose",
      "multusAutoconfigDir": "/host/etc/cni/net.d",
      "multusConfigFile": "auto",
      "readinessindicatorfile": "/host/etc/cni/net.d/10-ovn-kubernetes.conf"
    }
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: kube-multus-ds
  namespace: kube-system
  labels: {tier: node, app: multus}
spec:
  selector: {matchLabels: {app: multus}}
  updateStrategy: {type: RollingUpdate}
  template:
    metadata:
      labels: {tier: node, app: multus, name: multus}
      annotations: {pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce: privileged}
    spec:
      priorityClassName: system-node-critical
      hostNetwork: true
      tolerations: [{operator: Exists}]
      serviceAccountName: multus
      containers:
      - name: kube-multus
        image: ghcr.io/k8snetworkplumbingwg/multus-cni:v4.1.0-thick
        command: ["/usr/src/multus-cni/bin/multus-daemon","-config","/etc/cni/net.d/multus.d/daemon-config.json"]
        resources:
          requests: {cpu: 10m, memory: 64Mi}
          limits: {cpu: 100m, memory: 128Mi}
        securityContext: {privileged: true}
        volumeMounts:
          - {name: cni, mountPath: /host/etc/cni/net.d}
          - {name: cnibin, mountPath: /host/opt/cni/bin}
          - {name: multus-cfg, mountPath: /tmp/multus-conf}
          - {name: daemon-cfg, mountPath: /etc/cni/net.d/multus.d}
          - {name: host-run, mountPath: /host/run, mountPropagation: Bidirectional}
          - {name: host-var-lib-cni, mountPath: /host/var/lib/cni}
          # v17 FIX (pod-VF offload): the thick DAEMON runs each CNI delegate
          # in-process in ITS OWN mount ns, so it needs the host paths the
          # delegates touch mounted at their REAL host paths (not /host/-prefixed):
          #  1. device-plugin checkpoint — sriov delegate maps bf3_p0_vfs -> VF PCI
          #     (without it: "error reading kubelet_internal_checkpoint")
          #  2. /run/netns — daemon Statfs's the pod netns the shim passes
          #     (without it: "no net namespace /var/run/netns/cni-... found")
          #  3. /run/ovn-kubernetes — eth0 (OVN-K) delegate dials ovn-cni-server.sock
          #     (without it: "dial unix .../ovn-cni-server.sock: no such file")
          - {name: host-var-lib-kubelet-dp, mountPath: /var/lib/kubelet/device-plugins}
          - {name: host-run-netns, mountPath: /run/netns, mountPropagation: HostToContainer}
          - {name: host-run-ovn, mountPath: /run/ovn-kubernetes, mountPropagation: HostToContainer}
          # v17 FIX (#5): the whereabouts IPAM delegate (run in-process by this
          # daemon) hardcodes /etc/cni/net.d/whereabouts.d/whereabouts.{conf,kubeconfig};
          # in the daemon ns the host CNI dir is /host/etc/cni/net.d, so without this
          # bind the delegate errors "open .../whereabouts.kubeconfig: no such file".
          - {name: host-whereabouts-d, mountPath: /etc/cni/net.d/whereabouts.d}
      initContainers:
      - name: install-multus-binary
        image: ghcr.io/k8snetworkplumbingwg/multus-cni:v4.1.0-thick
        # v15 FIX: in multus-cni:v4.1.0-thick the install binary lives at
        # /usr/src/multus-cni/bin/install_multus, NOT /install_multus (the old
        # path StartError'd). Its ONLY valid flags are -t/--type and -d/--dest-dir;
        # the old --cni-conf-dir/--multus-conf-file are "unknown flag" (exit 2).
        # It just copies the multus binaries to the host CNI bin dir; the CNI conf
        # is auto-generated by the daemon (multusConfigFile: auto).
        command: ["/usr/src/multus-cni/bin/install_multus","--type","thick","--dest-dir","/host/opt/cni/bin"]
        resources:
          requests: {cpu: 10m, memory: 15Mi}
          limits: {cpu: 100m, memory: 64Mi}
        securityContext: {privileged: true}
        volumeMounts:
          - {name: cni, mountPath: /host/etc/cni/net.d}
          - {name: cnibin, mountPath: /host/opt/cni/bin}
          - {name: multus-cfg, mountPath: /tmp/multus-conf}
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 10
      volumes:
        - name: cni
          hostPath: {path: /etc/cni/net.d}
        - name: cnibin
          hostPath: {path: /opt/cni/bin}
        - name: multus-cfg
          configMap:
            name: multus-cni-config
            items: [{key: cni-conf.json, path: cni-conf.json}]
        - name: daemon-cfg
          configMap: {name: multus-daemon-config}
        - name: host-run
          hostPath: {path: /run, type: DirectoryOrCreate}
        - name: host-var-lib-cni
          hostPath: {path: /var/lib/cni, type: DirectoryOrCreate}
        - name: host-var-lib-kubelet-dp
          hostPath: {path: /var/lib/kubelet/device-plugins, type: DirectoryOrCreate}
        - name: host-run-netns
          hostPath: {path: /run/netns, type: DirectoryOrCreate}
        - name: host-run-ovn
          hostPath: {path: /run/ovn-kubernetes, type: DirectoryOrCreate}
        - name: host-whereabouts-d
          hostPath: {path: /etc/cni/net.d/whereabouts.d, type: DirectoryOrCreate}

manifests.name: host-ovnk-mtu-1400
manifests.content:
# v13 fix #2: force ovn-kubernetes/ovn-config CM.mtu = 1400.
# Palette pack default is 8940 which assumes MTU-9000 fabric; on brens1f0 that
# uplink is MTU 1500 so pods over the OVN-K path drop. Once we swap uplinks to
# the DPU data port (v13 offload path) we can go back to 8940, but until then
# 1400 keeps host-CNI pod networking honest.
# Idempotent Job runs after CNI comes up.
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: host-ovnk-mtu-1400
  namespace: kube-system
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: host-ovnk-mtu-1400
  namespace: ovn-kubernetes
rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: [configmaps]
    verbs: [get, patch, update]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: host-ovnk-mtu-1400
  namespace: ovn-kubernetes
roleRef: {apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io, kind: Role, name: host-ovnk-mtu-1400}
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: host-ovnk-mtu-1400
    namespace: kube-system
---
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: host-ovnk-mtu-1400
  namespace: kube-system
spec:
  schedule: "*/1 * * * *"
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 1
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 1
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      backoffLimit: 0
      template:
        spec:
          serviceAccountName: host-ovnk-mtu-1400
          restartPolicy: Never
          containers:
          - name: patch
            image: bitnami/kubectl:latest
            command: ["/bin/sh","-c"]
            args:
              - |
                set -eu
                kubectl -n ovn-kubernetes patch cm ovn-config \
                  --type=merge -p '{"data":{"mtu":"1400"}}' || true
            resources: {requests: {cpu: 5m, memory: 32Mi}}

manifests.name: host-ovnk-zone-labeler
manifests.content:
# v13 fix #1: aggressive k8s.ovn.org/zone-name labeler on HOST cluster nodes.
# Palette-installed OVN-K comes up without this label; ovnkube-controller
# crashes until the node has it. CronJob is too slow (min 1 min) and misses the
# first-boot race. DaemonSet self-labels the node it runs on, and keeps
# re-labelling every 15 s so a Palette-agent revert can't stick.
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: host-ovnk-zone-labeler
  namespace: kube-system
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: host-ovnk-zone-labeler
rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: [nodes]
    verbs: [get, list, patch, update]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: host-ovnk-zone-labeler
roleRef: {apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io, kind: ClusterRole, name: host-ovnk-zone-labeler}
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: host-ovnk-zone-labeler
    namespace: kube-system
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: host-ovnk-zone-labeler
  namespace: kube-system
spec:
  selector: {matchLabels: {app: host-ovnk-zone-labeler}}
  template:
    metadata: {labels: {app: host-ovnk-zone-labeler}}
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: host-ovnk-zone-labeler
      priorityClassName: system-node-critical
      hostNetwork: true
      tolerations: [{operator: Exists}]
      containers:
      - name: labeler
        image: bitnami/kubectl:latest
        env:
          - name: NODE_NAME
            valueFrom: {fieldRef: {fieldPath: spec.nodeName}}
        command: ["/bin/sh","-c"]
        args:
          - |
            set -eu
            while true; do
              kubectl label node "$NODE_NAME" "k8s.ovn.org/zone-name=$NODE_NAME" --overwrite >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
              sleep 15
            done
        resources: {requests: {cpu: 5m, memory: 32Mi}, limits: {cpu: 50m, memory: 64Mi}}

manifests.name: host-sriov-device-plugin
manifests.content:
# v14 HBN offload plumbing #3: SR-IOV device plugin on host cluster.
# Advertises enp14s0f0np0's VFs as `nvidia.com/bf3_p0_vfs`.
#
# v14 fixes vs v13:
# - Same nodeSelector fix as host-bf3-sriov-vfs (Palette doesn't set
#   node-role.kubernetes.io/worker — use nodeAffinity NotIn control-plane).
# - Selector simplified to only pfNames — the v13 (vendors+devices+drivers+
#   pfNames#range) combo caused DP to register 0 devices even though VFs matched;
#   netDeviceProvider's filter chain rejects them in that combo. Just pfNames
#   works, and the DP will discover whatever VF count the host DS created.
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata: {name: sriov-device-plugin, namespace: kube-system}
---
# v17 FIX (#4, durable): the config.json is GENERATED at runtime by an init
# container (detect-pf) that discovers PF0 by phys_port_name=p0, instead of
# hardcoding pfNames: ["eth0"]. The PF netdev name is non-deterministic across
# reprovisions, so a static name breaks VF discovery (0 devices advertised). No
# ConfigMap needed — the init writes /etc/pcidp/config.json into a shared emptyDir.
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: sriov-device-plugin
  namespace: kube-system
  labels: {app: sriov-device-plugin}
spec:
  selector: {matchLabels: {name: sriov-device-plugin}}
  updateStrategy: {type: RollingUpdate}
  template:
    metadata:
      labels: {name: sriov-device-plugin, tier: node}
      annotations: {pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce: privileged}
    spec:
      hostNetwork: true
      hostPID: true
      priorityClassName: system-node-critical
      tolerations: [{operator: Exists}]
      affinity:
        nodeAffinity:
          requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
            nodeSelectorTerms:
              - matchExpressions:
                  - key: node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane
                    operator: DoesNotExist
      serviceAccountName: sriov-device-plugin
      containers:
      # v17 FIX (#4): detect PF0 (phys_port_name=p0, SR-IOV PF) INLINE and write the
      # config to a writable path, then exec the plugin with --config-file. Done in
      # the main container (not an init+emptyDir, which shared unreliably here) so the
      # PF name is always the actual one, name-agnostic across reprovisions.
      - name: kube-sriovdp
        image: ghcr.io/k8snetworkplumbingwg/sriov-network-device-plugin:v3.7.0
        command: ["/bin/sh","-c"]
        args:
          - |
            set -u
            find_pf() {
              for d in /sys/class/net/*; do
                [ -e "$d/phys_port_name" ] || continue
                [ "$(cat "$d/phys_port_name" 2>/dev/null)" = "p0" ] || continue
                tv=$(cat "$d/device/sriov_totalvfs" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
                [ "$tv" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null || continue
                basename "$d"; return 0
              done
              return 1
            }
            PF=""; i=0
            while [ -z "$PF" ] && [ "$i" -lt 60 ]; do PF=$(find_pf || true); [ -z "$PF" ] && sleep 5; i=$((i+1)); done
            [ -n "$PF" ] || { echo "PF0 (phys_port_name=p0, SR-IOV) not found"; exit 1; }
            mkdir -p /tmp/pcidp
            printf '{"resourceList":[{"resourceName":"bf3_p0_vfs","resourcePrefix":"nvidia.com","selectors":{"pfNames":["%s"]}}]}' "$PF" > /tmp/pcidp/config.json
            echo "detected PF0=$PF; config:"; cat /tmp/pcidp/config.json; echo
            exec /usr/bin/sriovdp --config-file /tmp/pcidp/config.json -logtostderr -v 5
        securityContext: {privileged: true}
        resources: {requests: {cpu: 25m, memory: 40Mi}, limits: {cpu: 250m, memory: 200Mi}}
        volumeMounts:
          - {name: devicesock, mountPath: /var/lib/kubelet/, mountPropagation: Bidirectional}
          - {name: log, mountPath: /var/log}
          - {name: sysfs, mountPath: /sys}
          - {name: device-info, mountPath: /var/run/k8s.cni.cncf.io/devinfo/dp, mountPropagation: Bidirectional}
      volumes:
        - {name: devicesock, hostPath: {path: /var/lib/kubelet/}}
        - {name: log, hostPath: {path: /var/log}}
        - {name: sysfs, hostPath: {path: /sys}}
        - {name: device-info, hostPath: {path: /var/run/k8s.cni.cncf.io/devinfo/dp, type: DirectoryOrCreate}}

manifests.name: host-whereabouts
manifests.content:
# v17 FIX (#5, durable): cluster-wide IPAM for the pod-VF flat /24.
#
# The hbn-fabric NAD used ipam type host-local, which allocates per-node with NO
# shared state — so a pod on gpu-sm01 and a pod on gpu-sm02 BOTH got the range
# start (10.10.11.10), colliding on the flat EVPN /24. whereabouts stores its
# allocations in cluster CRDs (via the k8s API), so every pod across every node
# gets a distinct IP. This installs whereabouts (CRDs + RBAC + the CNI binary +
# the ip-reconciler) on the host cluster; the NAD switches ipam -> whereabouts.
apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1
kind: CustomResourceDefinition
metadata:
  name: ippools.whereabouts.cni.cncf.io
spec:
  group: whereabouts.cni.cncf.io
  names: {kind: IPPool, plural: ippools, singular: ippool}
  scope: Namespaced
  versions:
    - name: v1alpha1
      served: true
      storage: true
      schema:
        openAPIV3Schema:
          type: object
          properties:
            spec:
              type: object
              description: IPPool spec
              properties:
                range: {type: string}
                allocations:
                  type: object
                  x-kubernetes-preserve-unknown-fields: true
---
apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1
kind: CustomResourceDefinition
metadata:
  name: overlappingrangeipreservations.whereabouts.cni.cncf.io
spec:
  group: whereabouts.cni.cncf.io
  names:
    kind: OverlappingRangeIPReservation
    plural: overlappingrangeipreservations
    singular: overlappingrangeipreservation
  scope: Namespaced
  versions:
    - name: v1alpha1
      served: true
      storage: true
      schema:
        openAPIV3Schema:
          type: object
          required: [spec]
          properties:
            spec:
              type: object
              required: [podref]
              properties:
                containerid: {type: string}
                podref: {type: string}
                ifname: {type: string}
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata: {name: whereabouts, namespace: kube-system}
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata: {name: whereabouts-cni}
rules:
  - apiGroups: ["whereabouts.cni.cncf.io"]
    resources: [ippools, overlappingrangeipreservations]
    verbs: [get, list, watch, create, update, patch, delete]
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: [pods]
    verbs: [get, list, watch]
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: [nodes]
    verbs: [get, list, watch]
  - apiGroups: ["k8s.cni.cncf.io"]
    resources: [network-attachment-definitions]
    verbs: [get, list, watch]
  - apiGroups: ["coordination.k8s.io"]
    resources: [leases]
    verbs: [get, list, watch, create, update, patch, delete]
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: [events]
    verbs: [create, patch, update]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata: {name: whereabouts-cni}
roleRef: {apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io, kind: ClusterRole, name: whereabouts-cni}
subjects: [{kind: ServiceAccount, name: whereabouts, namespace: kube-system}]
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: whereabouts
  namespace: kube-system
  labels: {app: whereabouts}
spec:
  selector: {matchLabels: {app: whereabouts}}
  updateStrategy: {type: RollingUpdate}
  template:
    metadata:
      labels: {app: whereabouts}
      annotations: {pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce: privileged}
    spec:
      hostNetwork: true
      priorityClassName: system-node-critical
      serviceAccountName: whereabouts
      tolerations: [{operator: Exists}]
      containers:
      - name: whereabouts
        image: ghcr.io/k8snetworkplumbingwg/whereabouts:v0.7.0
        command: ["/bin/sh"]
        args:
          - -c
          - |
            SLEEP=false /install-cni.sh && /ip-control-loop -log-level verbose
        env:
          - name: WHEREABOUTS_NAMESPACE
            valueFrom: {fieldRef: {fieldPath: metadata.namespace}}
          - name: NODENAME
            valueFrom: {fieldRef: {fieldPath: spec.nodeName}}
        securityContext: {privileged: true}
        resources: {requests: {cpu: 10m, memory: 32Mi}, limits: {cpu: 100m, memory: 128Mi}}
        volumeMounts:
          - {name: cnibin, mountPath: /host/opt/cni/bin}
          - {name: cni-net-dir, mountPath: /host/etc/cni/net.d}
      volumes:
        - name: cnibin
          hostPath: {path: /opt/cni/bin}
        - name: cni-net-dir
          hostPath: {path: /etc/cni/net.d}

manifests.name: kamaji-replicas-2
manifests.content:
# v13 fix #3: force TenantControlPlane.spec.controlPlane.deployment.replicas = 2.
# DPF's kamaji-cm creates the TCP with 3 replicas by default. The CP VM is
# CPU-oversubscribed (palette-controller-manager+dpf-provisioning eat the
# budget) so the 3rd kamaji pod stays Pending. TCP's Deployment then reports
# MinimumReplicasUnavailable, kamaji-cm silent-stalls on "missing NodePort for
# kube-apiserver", DPUs are stuck in Initializing on
# "DPUClusterNotAllocated: cluster ... phase: Creating", and the DPF DPU state
# machine won't advance. Reducing to 2 unblocks the whole cascade.
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: kamaji-replicas-2
  namespace: kube-system
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: kamaji-replicas-2
rules:
  - apiGroups: [kamaji.clastix.io]
    resources: [tenantcontrolplanes]
    verbs: [get, list, watch, patch, update]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: kamaji-replicas-2
roleRef: {apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io, kind: ClusterRole, name: kamaji-replicas-2}
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: kamaji-replicas-2
    namespace: kube-system
---
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: kamaji-replicas-2
  namespace: kube-system
spec:
  schedule: "*/1 * * * *"
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 1
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 1
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      backoffLimit: 0
      template:
        spec:
          serviceAccountName: kamaji-replicas-2
          restartPolicy: Never
          containers:
          - name: patch
            image: bitnami/kubectl:latest
            command: ["/bin/sh","-c"]
            args:
              - |
                set -eu
                # Every TenantControlPlane owned by DPF gets patched.
                for tcp in $(kubectl get tenantcontrolplane -A -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.namespace}/{.metadata.name}{"\n"}{end}' 2>/dev/null); do
                  ns="${tcp%/*}"; name="${tcp#*/}"
                  cur=$(kubectl -n "$ns" get tenantcontrolplane "$name" -o jsonpath='{.spec.controlPlane.deployment.replicas}' 2>/dev/null)
                  if [ "$cur" != "1" ]; then
                    kubectl -n "$ns" patch tenantcontrolplane "$name" \
                      --type=json \
                      -p '[{"op":"replace","path":"/spec/controlPlane/deployment/replicas","value":1}]' \
                      && echo "patched $ns/$name" || true
                  fi
                done
            resources: {requests: {cpu: 5m, memory: 32Mi}}

manifests.name: kamaji-vip-on-brex
manifests.content:
# v13 fix #4: put the Kamaji VIP on the OVN-K br-ex interface (host cluster).
# DPUCluster's keepalived listens on the physical uplink (e.g. ens18/ens1f0/
# enp0s3), but OVN-K wraps that as a slave of br-ex (brens*). ARP for the VIP
# then answers off the wrong interface and the DPUs' kube-proxy → NodePort
# route to Kamaji hangs. Adding the VIP as a /32 kernel IP on br-ex forces the
# host to answer ARP for it directly and makes kube-proxy's PREROUTING DNAT
# work — same DPU→Kamaji fix from
# feedback_ovnk_nodeport_bridge_fix / mtu9000_hbn_accel_achieved.
# Runs on CP nodes only (nodeSelector), auto-detects the OVN-K br-ex.
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: kamaji-vip-on-brex
  namespace: kube-system
spec:
  selector: {matchLabels: {app: kamaji-vip-on-brex}}
  template:
    metadata:
      labels: {app: kamaji-vip-on-brex}
      annotations:
        pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce: privileged
    spec:
      priorityClassName: system-node-critical
      nodeSelector:
        node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane: ""
      tolerations: [{operator: Exists}]
      hostNetwork: true
      hostPID: true
      containers:
      - name: vip-fixer
        image: busybox:1.36
        env:
          - name: VIP
            # Matches the profile default; if a deployment sets a different
            # controlPlaneVIP variable the operator can `kubectl set env` it.
            value: "172.16.30.240"
        command: ["sh","-c"]
        args:
          - |
            set -eu
            # busybox has ip built-in; no apk needed
            while true; do
              # Auto-detect the OVN-K br-ex: the interface whose name starts
              # with "br" and owns the node's primary route (172.16.30.0/24).
              BR=$(ip -o -4 addr show scope global | \
                    awk '/^[0-9]+: br/ {split($2,a,"@"); print a[1]}' | head -1)
              if [ -n "${BR:-}" ]; then
                ip addr add "$VIP/32" dev "$BR" 2>/dev/null || true
              fi
              sleep 30
            done
        securityContext: {privileged: true}
        resources: {requests: {cpu: 5m, memory: 64Mi}, limits: {cpu: 50m, memory: 64Mi}}

manifests.name: maas-dpu-network-setup
manifests.content:
---
# Reserves static-IP MaaS leases for each DPU's oob_net0, so the DPU gets a stable IP
# on first boot after BFB flash. Bypasses MaaS's default behaviour of ignoring
# DHCP requests from unknown MACs.
#
# What it does, per reconcile:
#   1. Loads MaaS OAuth creds from Secret maas-api-credentials (created below from spectro.var.*).
#   2. Shrinks the MaaS dynamic range on the mgmt subnet so the DPU-target IPs
#      are static-eligible (moves the boundary; leaves rest of the dynamic range intact).
#   3. Enumerates DPUDevice CRs — each has a bmcIp we can ARP-resolve into a BMC MAC.
#      For BF-3, the DPU OOB MAC == BMC MAC minus 1 in the last byte (verified: BMC
#      c4:70:bd:2b:f6:b7 → OOB c4:70:bd:2b:f6:b6; BMC c4:70:bd:f0:65:eb → OOB ...:ea).
#   4. Creates/updates a MaaS 'device' record for that OOB MAC with a STATIC IP
#      assignment on the mgmt subnet.
#   5. Refuses to allocate any IP already answering to ARP (safety).
#
# Idempotent. Records success via annotation on the DPUDevice.
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: maas-dpu-network-setup
rules:
  - apiGroups: ["provisioning.dpu.nvidia.com"]
    resources: ["dpudevices", "dpunodes", "dpudiscoveries"]
    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "patch", "update"]
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["secrets"]
    resourceNames: ["maas-api-credentials"]
    verbs: ["get"]
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: maas-dpu-network-setup
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: maas-dpu-network-setup
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: maas-dpu-network-setup
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: maas-dpu-network-setup
    namespace: dpf-operator-system
---
# Templated Secret — cluster-create supplies the values.
# NOTE: {{ .spectro.var.MaasApiEndpoint }} etc are resolved by Palette at deploy time.
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: maas-api-credentials
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
type: Opaque
stringData:
  endpoint: "{{ .spectro.var.MaasApiEndpoint }}"
  consumer_key: "{{ .spectro.var.MaasConsumerKey }}"
  token_key: "{{ .spectro.var.MaasTokenKey }}"
  token_secret: "{{ .spectro.var.MaasTokenSecret }}"
  subnet_cidr: "{{ .spectro.var.MgmtSubnetCidr }}"
  dpu1_oob_ip: "{{ .spectro.var.Dpu1OobIp }}"
  dpu2_oob_ip: "{{ .spectro.var.Dpu2OobIp }}"
---
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: maas-dpu-network-setup
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
spec:
  schedule: "*/2 * * * *"
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 1
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 3
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      backoffLimit: 1
      ttlSecondsAfterFinished: 900
      template:
        spec:
          serviceAccountName: maas-dpu-network-setup
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
          hostNetwork: true
          affinity:
            nodeAffinity:
              requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
                nodeSelectorTerms:
                  - matchExpressions:
                      - key: node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane
                        operator: Exists
          tolerations:
            - key: node-role.kubernetes.io/control-plane
              operator: Exists
              effect: NoSchedule
            - key: node-role.kubernetes.io/master
              operator: Exists
              effect: NoSchedule
          containers:
            - name: setup
              image: python:3.12-slim
              command: ["/bin/bash","-c"]
              args:
                - |
                  set -euo pipefail
                  export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
                  apt-get update -qq && apt-get install -y -qq iproute2 iputils-arping curl >/dev/null
                  pip install --quiet requests-oauthlib >/dev/null
                  # install kubectl (script needs it to read DPUDevice CRs)
                  KV=$(curl -sk https://kubernetes.default.svc/version | grep -oE '"gitVersion":\s*"[^"]+"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
                  curl -sL "https://dl.k8s.io/release/${KV:-v1.34.2}/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl" -o /usr/local/bin/kubectl
                  chmod +x /usr/local/bin/kubectl
                  python3 /scripts/setup.py
              env:
                - name: MAAS_ENDPOINT
                  valueFrom: { secretKeyRef: { name: maas-api-credentials, key: endpoint } }
                - name: MAAS_CONSUMER_KEY
                  valueFrom: { secretKeyRef: { name: maas-api-credentials, key: consumer_key } }
                - name: MAAS_TOKEN_KEY
                  valueFrom: { secretKeyRef: { name: maas-api-credentials, key: token_key } }
                - name: MAAS_TOKEN_SECRET
                  valueFrom: { secretKeyRef: { name: maas-api-credentials, key: token_secret } }
                - name: MGMT_SUBNET_CIDR
                  valueFrom: { secretKeyRef: { name: maas-api-credentials, key: subnet_cidr } }
                - name: DPU1_OOB_IP
                  valueFrom: { secretKeyRef: { name: maas-api-credentials, key: dpu1_oob_ip } }
                - name: DPU2_OOB_IP
                  valueFrom: { secretKeyRef: { name: maas-api-credentials, key: dpu2_oob_ip } }
              volumeMounts:
                - name: scripts
                  mountPath: /scripts
                  readOnly: true
          volumes:
            - name: scripts
              configMap:
                name: maas-dpu-network-setup-scripts
                defaultMode: 0755
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: maas-dpu-network-setup-scripts
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
data:
  setup.py: |
    #!/usr/bin/env python3
    """Reserve static-IP MaaS leases for each DPUDevice's OOB MAC.

    Runs on the CP node's host network so we can arping the BMC on the mgmt LAN
    to learn its MAC directly. The OOB MAC on BF-3 is BMC-MAC minus 1 in the
    last byte — deterministic.
    """
    import os
    import subprocess
    import sys
    import ipaddress
    import time
    from urllib.parse import urlencode, urljoin
    from urllib.request import Request, urlopen
    from urllib.error import HTTPError

    ENDPOINT = os.environ["MAAS_ENDPOINT"].rstrip("/")
    CK = os.environ["MAAS_CONSUMER_KEY"]
    TK = os.environ["MAAS_TOKEN_KEY"]
    TS = os.environ["MAAS_TOKEN_SECRET"]
    SUBNET_CIDR = os.environ["MGMT_SUBNET_CIDR"]
    # Mapping "<dpu-serial>=<ip>" pairs from env; example DPU1_OOB_IP=172.16.30.29.
    # We index by discovery order (first DPUDevice returned == DPU1).
    DPU_IPS = [os.environ.get("DPU1_OOB_IP"), os.environ.get("DPU2_OOB_IP")]

    def maas_auth_header(nonce=None):
        # MaaS accepts OAuth PLAINTEXT signatures.
        import random
        n = nonce or random.randint(1, 999999)
        t = int(time.time())
        return (
            'OAuth oauth_version="1.0", '
            'oauth_signature_method="PLAINTEXT", '
            f'oauth_consumer_key="{CK}", oauth_token="{TK}", '
            f'oauth_signature="&{TS}", '
            f'oauth_nonce="{n}", oauth_timestamp="{t}"'
        )

    def maas(path, method="GET", data=None):
        import json as _json
        url = f"{ENDPOINT}/{path.lstrip('/')}"
        headers = {"Authorization": maas_auth_header()}
        body = None
        if data is not None:
            body = urlencode(data, doseq=True).encode()
            headers["Content-Type"] = "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"
        req = Request(url, data=body, method=method, headers=headers)
        try:
            r = urlopen(req, timeout=15)
            raw = r.read().decode()
            return r.status, _json.loads(raw) if raw and raw[0] in "[{" else raw
        except HTTPError as e:
            return e.code, e.read().decode()

    def arp_lookup(ip: str) -> str:
        """Return the MAC address answering to `ip` on the current host, or ''."""
        subprocess.run(["arping", "-c", "1", "-w", "2", "-I", "0", ip],
                       capture_output=True, check=False)
        # ip neigh show <ip>
        r = subprocess.run(["ip", "neigh", "show", ip],
                           capture_output=True, text=True, check=False)
        for line in r.stdout.splitlines():
            parts = line.split()
            for i, p in enumerate(parts):
                if p == "lladdr" and i + 1 < len(parts):
                    return parts[i + 1].lower()
        return ""

    def derive_oob_mac(bmc_mac: str) -> str:
        octets = bmc_mac.lower().split(":")
        last = int(octets[-1], 16)
        octets[-1] = f"{(last - 1) & 0xff:02x}"
        return ":".join(octets)

    def get_dpu_bmcs():
        """Return [(dpudevice_name, bmc_ip), ...] via kubectl (in-cluster kubeconfig)."""
        import json as _json
        r = subprocess.run(
            ["kubectl", "-n", "dpf-operator-system", "get", "dpudevice", "-o", "json"],
            capture_output=True, text=True, check=True)
        d = _json.loads(r.stdout)
        out = []
        for item in d.get("items", []):
            name = item["metadata"]["name"]
            bmc = item.get("spec", {}).get("bmcIp", "")
            if bmc:
                out.append((name, bmc))
        return sorted(out)

    def shrink_dynamic_range(want_ips):
        """If any DPU-target IP is inside a dynamic range, shrink that range to exclude it.

        Only moves the low boundary; keeps range name/vlan intact.
        """
        code, ranges = maas("ipranges/")
        if code != 200:
            print(f"list ipranges: HTTP {code}: {ranges}", file=sys.stderr)
            return
        target_net = ipaddress.ip_network(SUBNET_CIDR)
        for r in ranges:
            if r.get("type") != "dynamic":
                continue
            start = ipaddress.ip_address(r["start_ip"])
            end = ipaddress.ip_address(r["end_ip"])
            if start not in target_net:
                continue
            need_shift = max([ipaddress.ip_address(ip) for ip in want_ips if ip and start <= ipaddress.ip_address(ip) <= end],
                             default=None)
            if need_shift is None:
                continue
            new_start = ipaddress.ip_address(int(need_shift) + 1)
            if new_start > end:
                print(f"cannot shrink range id={r['id']} — would go empty", file=sys.stderr)
                continue
            print(f"shrinking range id={r['id']} from {start}-{end} to {new_start}-{end}")
            maas(f"ipranges/{r['id']}/", method="PUT",
                 data={"start_ip": str(new_start), "end_ip": str(end)})

    def find_or_create_device(hostname: str, mac: str, ip: str):
        """Ensure a MaaS device exists with the given MAC and STATIC ip."""
        code, devs = maas("devices/")
        if code != 200:
            print(f"list devices: HTTP {code}", file=sys.stderr)
            return False
        existing = next(
            (d for d in devs
             if any(i.get("mac_address", "").lower() == mac
                    for i in d.get("interface_set", []))),
            None)
        if existing is None:
            code, r = maas("devices/", method="POST",
                           data={"hostname": hostname, "mac_addresses": mac,
                                 "domain": "maas-custeng.sc"})
            if code not in (200, 201):
                print(f"create device: HTTP {code}: {r}", file=sys.stderr)
                return False
            import json as _json
            existing = _json.loads(r) if isinstance(r, str) else r
            print(f"created device {hostname} sysid={existing.get('system_id')}")
        sysid = existing["system_id"]
        iface = existing["interface_set"][0]
        # Unlink any existing links
        for link in iface.get("links", []) or []:
            maas(f"nodes/{sysid}/interfaces/{iface['id']}/?op=unlink_subnet",
                 method="POST", data={"id": link["id"]})
        # Link STATIC
        subnet_id = None
        code, subnets = maas("subnets/")
        if code == 200:
            for s in subnets:
                if s.get("cidr") == SUBNET_CIDR:
                    subnet_id = s["id"]; break
        if not subnet_id:
            print(f"cannot find subnet id for {SUBNET_CIDR}", file=sys.stderr)
            return False
        code, r = maas(f"nodes/{sysid}/interfaces/{iface['id']}/?op=link_subnet",
                       method="POST",
                       data={"mode": "STATIC", "subnet": str(subnet_id),
                             "ip_address": ip})
        if code == 200:
            print(f"linked {hostname}/{mac} → {ip}")
            return True
        print(f"link_subnet: HTTP {code}: {r}", file=sys.stderr)
        return False

    def already_configured(dpudevice_name: str) -> bool:
        r = subprocess.run(
            ["kubectl", "-n", "dpf-operator-system", "get", "dpudevice", dpudevice_name,
             "-o", "jsonpath={.metadata.annotations.dpf-testing\\.custeng\\.sc/maas-static-ip-assigned}"],
            capture_output=True, text=True, check=False)
        return bool(r.stdout.strip())

    def mark_configured(dpudevice_name: str, ip: str):
        subprocess.run(
            ["kubectl", "-n", "dpf-operator-system", "annotate", "dpudevice",
             dpudevice_name,
             f"dpf-testing.custeng.sc/maas-static-ip-assigned={ip}", "--overwrite"],
            check=False)

    def main():
        want = [ip for ip in DPU_IPS if ip]
        if not want:
            print("no DPU IPs configured (DPU{1,2}_OOB_IP empty); nothing to do")
            return
        shrink_dynamic_range(want)
        bmcs = get_dpu_bmcs()
        if not bmcs:
            print("no DPUDevice CRs found yet")
            return
        for (name, bmc_ip), want_ip in zip(bmcs, DPU_IPS):
            if not want_ip:
                continue
            if already_configured(name):
                print(f"{name} already configured; skip")
                continue
            bmc_mac = arp_lookup(bmc_ip)
            if not bmc_mac:
                print(f"{name}: BMC {bmc_ip} not in ARP; skipping this pass")
                continue
            oob_mac = derive_oob_mac(bmc_mac)
            hostname = name.replace("dpu-device-", "").replace("dpu-node-", "")
            hostname = f"{hostname}-oob"
            if find_or_create_device(hostname, oob_mac, want_ip):
                mark_configured(name, want_ip)

    if __name__ == "__main__":
        main()

manifests.name: tenant-bootstrap-token-generator
manifests.content:
# Companion to 02-tenant-bootstrap-token-keeper.yaml.
#
# The keeper EXTENDS existing bootstrap-token Secrets in the tenant cluster (so
# tokens already in DPU bfcfgs stay valid). But if no tokens exist (e.g., they
# were already deleted by tokencleaner before the keeper started), there is
# nothing to extend — the keeper does nothing and DPUs cannot join.
#
# This generator ensures there is ALWAYS at least one fresh, valid bootstrap
# token in the tenant cluster's kube-system namespace, so any new DPU coming
# online can fetch it via the cluster-info ConfigMap signed JWS.
#
# It runs every 30 minutes and:
#   1. Checks if at least one bootstrap-token-* Secret exists in kube-system
#      with expiration > 1 hour from now.
#   2. If yes, exits (the keeper will extend it).
#   3. If no, generates a new token (24h TTL), creates the Secret, which causes
#      kube-controller-manager's bootstrap-signer to publish a JWS in
#      kube-public/cluster-info.
#
# This prevents the failure mode where:
#   - Keeper has been crashing (e.g., bash-vs-sh image bug).
#   - All tokens expire and tokencleaner deletes them.
#   - Now there are NO tokens for new DPUs to join with.
#
# Why: belt-and-suspenders against token starvation. Pair with the keeper.
# How to apply: deploys with the cluster profile; runs every 30 min, idempotent.
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: dpf-bootstrap-token-generator
rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["secrets"]
    verbs: ["get", "list", "create"]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: dpf-bootstrap-token-generator-tenant-kubeconfig
  namespace: dpu-cplane-tenant1
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: dpf-bootstrap-token-keeper-secret-reader
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: dpf-bootstrap-token-generator
    namespace: dpf-operator-system
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: dpf-bootstrap-token-generator
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
---
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: dpf-bootstrap-token-generator
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
spec:
  schedule: "*/30 * * * *"
  concurrencyPolicy: Forbid
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 1
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 2
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      backoffLimit: 1
      ttlSecondsAfterFinished: 1800
      template:
        spec:
          serviceAccountName: dpf-bootstrap-token-generator
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
          containers:
            - name: gen
              image: bitnami/kubectl:latest
              command:
                - /bin/bash
                - -c
                - |
                  set -euo pipefail

                  echo "[$(date -u +%FT%TZ)] dpf-bootstrap-token-generator run"

                  # Pull tenant admin kubeconfig from host cluster Secret.
                  kubectl -n dpu-cplane-tenant1 get secret dpu-cplane-tenant1-admin-kubeconfig \
                    -o jsonpath='{.data.admin\.conf}' | base64 -d > /tmp/tenant.kubeconfig
                  export KUBECONFIG=/tmp/tenant.kubeconfig

                  # Find any bootstrap token with at least 1 hour of life left.
                  THRESHOLD=$(date -u -d '+1 hour' +%s)
                  HAS_VALID=0
                  for s in $(kubectl -n kube-system get secret -o name 2>/dev/null | grep '^secret/bootstrap-token-' || true); do
                    exp=$(kubectl -n kube-system get $s -o jsonpath='{.data.expiration}' 2>/dev/null | base64 -d || echo "")
                    [ -z "$exp" ] && continue
                    exp_epoch=$(date -u -d "$exp" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
                    if [ "$exp_epoch" -gt "$THRESHOLD" ]; then
                      HAS_VALID=1
                      echo "  $s expires $exp (>1h ahead) — sufficient"
                      break
                    fi
                  done

                  if [ "$HAS_VALID" = "1" ]; then
                    echo "  at least one valid token already exists — nothing to do"
                    exit 0
                  fi

                  echo "  no valid token found, generating fresh one"
                  TID=$(head -c 6 /dev/urandom | od -A n -t x1 | tr -d ' \n' | head -c 6)
                  TSEC=$(head -c 8 /dev/urandom | od -A n -t x1 | tr -d ' \n' | head -c 16)
                  EXP=$(date -u -d '+24 hours' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)

                  cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
                  apiVersion: v1
                  kind: Secret
                  metadata:
                    name: bootstrap-token-${TID}
                    namespace: kube-system
                    labels:
                      dpf.spectrocloud.com/auto-generated: "true"
                  type: bootstrap.kubernetes.io/token
                  stringData:
                    description: "DPF auto-generated (no valid token found)"
                    token-id: "${TID}"
                    token-secret: "${TSEC}"
                    expiration: "${EXP}"
                    usage-bootstrap-authentication: "true"
                    usage-bootstrap-signing: "true"
                    auth-extra-groups: "system:bootstrappers:kubeadm:default-node-token"
                  EOF
                  echo "  created bootstrap-token-${TID}, expires ${EXP}"
                  echo "[$(date -u +%FT%TZ)] done"

manifests.name: dms-api-hosts-fixer
manifests.content:
# Durable fix for the DPU-join blocker caused by the Palette OVN-K gateway defect.
#
# ROOT CAUSE: the per-DPU DMS pods (gpu-smNN-dms) are hostNetwork with
# dnsPolicy=ClusterFirstWithHostNet, so they resolve via CoreDNS (10.96.0.10).
# But on this Palette OVN-K build, hostNetwork pods on the WORKER nodes cannot
# reach ClusterIP services (the OVN gateway masquerade path is broken) -> the
# DMS times out resolving the host cluster's API endpoint
# (dpf-...-<id>.maas-custeng.sc) -> it can never register the DPUDevice ->
# both DPUs sit at "DPU Cluster Config" forever and never join.
#
# The HOST itself CAN resolve that endpoint (via the MaaS upstream DNS in the
# host resolv.conf) -- only the CoreDNS path from the pod netns is dead. So this
# DaemonSet resolves the API endpoint the way the host does and injects the
# mapping straight into each DMS container's /etc/hosts (via nsenter), which
# short-circuits the broken CoreDNS lookup. Re-runs every 20s so freshly-created
# DMS pods (each DPU re-provision spawns a new one) get fixed automatically.
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: dms-api-hosts-fixer
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: dms-api-hosts-fixer
rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: [endpoints, nodes]
    verbs: [get, list]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: dms-api-hosts-fixer
roleRef: {apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io, kind: ClusterRole, name: dms-api-hosts-fixer}
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: dms-api-hosts-fixer
    namespace: dpf-operator-system
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: dms-api-hosts-fixer
  namespace: dpf-operator-system
spec:
  selector: {matchLabels: {app: dms-api-hosts-fixer}}
  template:
    metadata: {labels: {app: dms-api-hosts-fixer}}
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: dms-api-hosts-fixer
      hostNetwork: true
      hostPID: true
      dnsPolicy: Default          # use the HOST resolv.conf (MaaS upstream), which CAN resolve the API endpoint
      priorityClassName: system-node-critical
      tolerations: [{operator: Exists}]
      containers:
      - name: fixer
        image: bitnami/kubectl:latest
        securityContext:
          privileged: true
          runAsUser: 0
        command: ["/bin/sh","-c"]
        args:
          - |
            set +e
            while true; do
              # API endpoint hostname the DMS uses = the worker kubelet's server.
              URL=$(grep -h 'server:' /host/etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $2}' | head -1)
              HOST=$(echo "$URL" | sed 's|https\?://||; s|:.*||')
              # CP apiserver IP from the kubernetes Endpoints (kubectl uses the in-cluster
              # 10.96.0.1, which IS reachable from hostNetwork -- the apiserver is host-network
              # backed, unlike CoreDNS whose ClusterIP is dead through the OVN gateway defect).
              IP=$(kubectl get endpoints kubernetes -n default -o jsonpath='{.subsets[0].addresses[0].ip}' 2>/dev/null)
              case "$HOST" in ""|*[0-9].[0-9]*.[0-9]*.[0-9]*) HOST="" ;; esac
              if [ -n "$HOST" ] && [ -n "$IP" ]; then
                # A DMS pod dir has a containers/hostagent subdir. Its /etc/hosts is a bind-mount
                # from <poddir>/etc-hosts, so appending to that file shows up inside the DMS pod.
                for pd in /host/var/lib/kubelet/pods/*/; do
                  [ -d "$pd/containers/hostagent" ] || continue
                  eh="$pd/etc-hosts"
                  [ -f "$eh" ] || continue
                  grep -q " $HOST\$" "$eh" 2>/dev/null || echo "$IP $HOST" >> "$eh"
                done
              fi
              sleep 20
            done
        volumeMounts:
        - {name: host, mountPath: /host}
        resources: {requests: {cpu: 10m, memory: 64Mi}, limits: {cpu: 100m, memory: 256Mi}}
      volumes:
      - {name: host, hostPath: {path: /}}
